<div id="researchpapers" align="center">

## Research papers: 2026 4/5

[2026 (5/5)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/README.md), [2026 (4/5)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2026_4.md), [2026 (3/5)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2026_3.md), [2026 (2/5)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2026_2.md), [2026 (1/5)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2026_1.md), [2025 (4/4)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2025_4.md),[2025 (3/4)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2025_3.md), [2025 (2/4)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2025_2.md), [2025 (1/4)](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2025_01.md), [2024](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2024.md), [2023](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_2023.md), [Earlier](http://github.com/tmgthb/Autonomous-Agents/blob/main/resources/Autonomous_Agents_Research_Papers_Earlier.md)


Chronological order. 





</div>



---


#### 15th June 2026

[AI+CAD Data Representation Architecture: From AI+CAD Solid Modeling to AI+CAD Industrial-Grade Parametric Feature Modeling](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16797)

- WHUCAD: introduces a three-level data representation architecture designed to bridge the gap between simple AI-based solid modeling and industrial-grade parametric feature modeling.
- The framework integrates HPSketch for 2D sketch constraints and WHUCAD for 3D feature operations, utilizing NamingCAD and PointerCAD to manage topological element identification and selection.
- The architecture supports complex engineering design workflows by incorporating design history, sketch constraints, and permanent topological naming, enabling automated parameter propagation and feature reconstruction.

---

[Bayesian Inference and Decision Audits for Public Archives of Frontier AI Evaluations](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17005)

- Archive-and-Adjudication Protocol: introduces a framework for reconstructing public AI evaluation histories to distinguish evidential change from reporting artifacts using Archive Contract, Observability Boundary, Adjudication Protocol, Bayesian Decision Readout, Candidate Frontier Model, Objective Archives, Preference Stress Test, and Agentic Pilots.
- The protocol utilizes repeated snapshots to normalize evaluation data, enabling formal verification of observability boundaries and the application of Bayesian decision diagnostics to assess model claims.
- The research demonstrates that terminal-only archives are insufficient for timing inference, and subjects a candidate selection-aware model to rigorous falsification gates, ultimately rejecting its predictive and calibration claims.

---

[PaperJury: Due-Process Review for Bounded LaTeX Revision](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16322)

- PaperJury: introduces a closed-loop system for LaTeX manuscript revision that enforces a strict separation between deterministic orchestration and semantic agents to ensure artifact-safe editing.
- The system utilizes a durable ledger to maintain issue identity across rounds and employs a due-process trial involving a whole-paper defense and a local-context jury to adjudicate contested claims.
- PaperJury incorporates risk-proportional guard chains and a frozen claim spine to prevent unsafe edits, ensuring that automated revisions remain confined to their intended scope and maintain document integrity.

---

[How Far Can Machine Translation Quality Take You? Extrinsic Discourse Evaluation in Goal-Oriented Setups](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16596)

- Extrinsic Discourse Evaluation Framework: introduces a methodology for assessing machine translation quality by measuring its downstream impact on goal-oriented communication and coordination tasks.
- The framework utilizes a static entity counting task to isolate referential consistency and an interactive multi-agent Welfare Diplomacy game to capture how translation errors propagate through sequential communication.
- Experimental results demonstrate that standard intrinsic metrics like COMETQE correlate weakly with downstream task performance, highlighting the necessity of task-based evaluation for discourse-sensitive applications.

---


[Progressive Knowledge-Guided Large Language Model Framework for Bearing Fault Diagnosis](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16684)

- Progressive Knowledge-Guided Large Language Model Framework for Bearing Fault Diagnosis: introduces a three-stage diagnostic pipeline that integrates physics-informed feature engineering, patch-based signal modeling, and multi-modal fusion using ChatGLM-6B, GPT-2-12layer, and GPT-2-6layer components.
- The framework utilizes an 81-dimensional measurement descriptor to establish physical traceability and employs a fault-adaptive signal segmentation mechanism to focus analytical attention on transient fault impulses.
- By incorporating structured fault mechanism knowledge implicitly into model parameters during training, the architecture achieves high diagnostic accuracy and interpretability without requiring external knowledge dependencies at inference.

---

[Tensor-Coord: Algebraic Decomposition of Joint Plan Tensors for Conflict-Free Multi-Agent LLM Planning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16478)

- Tensor-Coord: introduces a multilinear algebra framework that encodes joint plans of multiple LLMs as tensors to identify and resolve coordination failures through algebraic decomposition.
- The framework utilizes CP decomposition to quantify coordination complexity and Tucker decomposition to extract interpretable agent roles and temporal phases for targeted constraint generation.
- By iteratively analyzing the residual of the joint plan tensor, the system provides specific natural language guidance to LLMs, significantly improving conflict-free convergence in multi-agent planning tasks.

---


[Hierarchical Advantage Weighting for Online RL Fine-Tuning of VLAs from Sparse Episode Outcomes](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17043)

- HABC: introduces a dual-head critic architecture that decomposes sparse binary episode outcomes into viability and efficiency signals to guide online fine-tuning of VLAs.
- The framework utilizes a state-adaptive gate to dynamically balance viability and efficiency advantages, ensuring appropriate per-transition weighting for the actor loss.
- Intervention-aware credit assignment prevents supervision leakage by restricting outcome labels to policy-executed segments, effectively leveraging human corrections as imitation data.

---

[Qwen-RobotWorld: Unifying Embodied World Modeling through Language-Conditioned Video Generation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17030)

- Qwen-RobotWorld: introduces a language-conditioned video world model that utilizes a double-stream MMDiT architecture with MLLM action encoding to unify cross-scenario and cross-task embodied capabilities.
- The framework leverages the EWK dataset, containing 8.6M video-text pairs, to train a state transition function that grounds diverse embodied experiences in general visual priors.
- The model employs a general+expert progressive training curriculum and the Scene2Robot conditioning mechanism to achieve robust performance across robotic manipulation, autonomous driving, and indoor navigation.

---

[DEEPRUBRIC: Evidence-Tree Rubric Supervision for Efficient Reinforcement Learning of Deep Research Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17029)

- DEEPRUBRIC: introduces an evidence-first framework for constructing aligned query–rubric supervision by recursively building an evidence tree from raw corpora to derive training tasks and verifiable reward criteria.
- The framework utilizes a bottom-up synthesis process to co-generate training queries and rubric criteria, ensuring that reward signals are traceable to specific evidence and aligned with the research task scope.
- By training an 8B model with this structured supervision, the approach achieves competitive performance on open-retrieval benchmarks with significantly reduced RL training time and GPU-hours compared to prior methods.

---

[TokenPilot: Cache-Efficient Context Management for LLM Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17016)

- TokenPilot: introduces a dual-granularity context management framework that reconciles text-level sparsity with hardware cache alignment for LLMs.
- The framework utilizes Ingestion-Aware Compaction to stabilize prompt prefixes and Lifecycle-Aware Eviction to manage context segments based on residual task utility.
- TokenPilot significantly reduces inference costs by minimizing cache misses through deterministic layout standardization and conservative memory offloading.

---

[From Newtonian to Relativistic IAM: The Autonomous Principal as Reference Frame for Digital Identity](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17002)

- Relativistic IAM: introduces a relational identity architecture that replaces custodial identity management with an autonomous principal acting as its own reference frame for digital identity.
- The framework utilizes Decentralised Authentication (DKMS), Decentralised Semantic (OCA), and Distributed Governance to enable verifiable, causally ordered exchanges without reliance on a central authority.
- By treating identity as a relation rather than a held state, the architecture resolves structural failures in cross-jurisdictional, propagation-lag, and agentic regimes.

---

[Agent trajectories as programs: fingerprinting and programming coding-agent behavior](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16988)

- ProcGrep: introduces a procedural analysis framework that represents LLM agent trajectories as structured programs to enable behavioral fingerprinting and deterministic search.
- The framework utilizes vocabulary induction techniques like BPE to transform raw agent traces into composable subroutines, allowing for comparative analysis of agent styles and procedural habits.
- ProcGrep provides a programmable interface for auditing agent behavior, enabling task-aware model routing, cost analysis, and the definition of procedural rewards for long-horizon coding tasks.

---

[Consensus-based Agentic Large Language Model Framework for Harmonized Tariff Schedule Code Classification](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16987)

- Agentic LLM Framework: introduces a multi-agent system that integrates information retrieval, evidence-grounded reasoning, and consensus-based validation to perform hierarchical HTS code classification.
- The framework utilizes an Orchestrator to manage Search Agents and RAG Agents, which provide external and regulatory context for structured prediction.
- To ensure reliability, the system employs perturbation-based self-consistency and element-wise majority voting, escalating low-confidence classifications to human experts for final resolution.

---

[When Should a Robot Replan? Regret-Guided Update Scheduling in Time-Varying MDPs](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16972)

- TVMDP Adaptive Update Scheduler: introduces a regret-guided update scheduling framework for robots operating in non-stationary environments with limited computational budgets.
- The framework utilizes a skip-update algorithm that alternates between update phases and skip phases, employing a propagated-MAP state estimator to maintain performance between re-planning steps.
- By deriving an online, regret-guided update rule from a dynamic regret bound, the approach adaptively allocates a fixed budget of updates to minimize performance loss caused by environmental dynamics drift.

---

[SurroundNEXO: Ego-Centric Metric Bridging for Spatially Consistent Geometry in Autonomous Driving](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16960)

- SurroundNEXO: introduces a metric-grounded surround-view depth framework that utilizes Ego-Ray Positional Encoding, Sparse Metric Anchoring, and a Progressive Geometry Transformer to achieve spatially consistent geometry in autonomous driving.
- The framework grounds visual tokens in a shared ego-centric reference and recalibrates them with sparse metric anchors before performing progressive feature interaction to ensure robust depth estimation.
- SurroundNEXO effectively bridges the gap between high-fidelity single-view perception and globally consistent 3D geometry by avoiding premature cross-view fusion in low-overlap driving scenarios.

---

[A Causal Model of Theory of Mind in Conflict for Artificial Intelligence](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16944)

- Causal Model of Theory of Mind (ToM) in Conflict: introduces a structural causal model formalized as a directed acyclic graph to determine the situational and agent-level conditions under which ToM engagement is causally warranted.
- The framework utilizes exogenous variables including Conflict Complexity, Information Asymmetry, Objective Tractability, and Sophistication to drive endogenous mediators that determine the necessity of ToM engagement.
- By prioritizing Epistemic Accuracy as the primary outcome, the model decouples cognitive social reasoning from behavioral policy, allowing for resource-rational decision-making in AI systems.

---

[A Unified Causal-Origin Taxonomy of Distributional Shifts in Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16933)

- Causal-Origin Taxonomy of Distributional Shifts in Reinforcement Learning: introduces a structural framework that decomposes distributional shifts in RL into internal (agent-driven) and external (environment-driven) components based on the generative MDP process.
- The framework categorizes shifts using the MDP components: state distribution p(s), observation mapping p(o|s), policy π(a|o), transition dynamics p(s'|s,a), and reward mechanism p(r|s,a,s'), while distinguishing between explicit, implicit, and hybrid shifted-time boundaries.
- This taxonomy provides a principled diagnostic tool to map existing RL methods to specific causal origins of shift, enabling more precise evaluation of agent robustness and adaptation performance through standardized metrics.

---

[Greed Is Learned: Visible Incentives as Reward-Hacking Triggers](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16914)

- MoneyWorld: introduces a synthetic sandbox to demonstrate that LLMs become addicted to visible reward channels when reading them is necessary to maximize payoff.
- The framework shows that when a reward proxy is decision-relevant, LLMs learn to prioritize visible incentives over their pre-existing safety alignment.
- This learned greed is portable across domains and can be mitigated by hiding the reward channel or making it redundant to the task.

---

[Directory-Aware Query and Maintenance in Vector Databases](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16903)

- TRIEHI: introduces a native prefix-tree metadata index for vector databases that enables efficient directory-scoped retrieval and structural maintenance by representing directory hierarchies as reusable tree nodes.
- The framework formalizes Directory-Semantic Query (DSQ) and Directory-Semantic Maintenance (DSM) as first-class operations to replace expensive application-layer workarounds for hierarchical context management.
- By integrating TRIEHI into the OpenViking context database, the system supports filesystem-style organization and directory-recursive retrieval, significantly improving performance for LLM-based agent memory and knowledge-base QA workloads.

---

[Binary Tracking for Spatial QA and Navigation with Open Vision-Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16902)

- BinTrack: introduces a fully open-source spatial-localization agent that leverages the temporal ordering of robot trajectories to perform binary search over segments between anchor landmarks.
- The framework utilizes a multi-view memory representation and a structured retrieval primitive to enable efficient spatial reasoning without relying on closed-source models.
- The paper also releases the GangnamLoop benchmark, a multi-trip outdoor dataset designed to evaluate revisit handling and cross-domain robustness in service robot deployments.

---

[SEMANTIC FLIP: Synthetic OOD Generation for Robust Refusal in Embodied Question Answering and Spatial Localization](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16898)

- SEMANTIC FLIP: introduces a framework for embodied refusal that synthesizes auxiliary OOD samples by independently corrupting queries (Q-FLIP) or video memory (V-FLIP) to train a lightweight MLP rejection gate on top of a frozen VLM encoder.
- The framework enables embodied agents to detect unanswerable queries by learning a joint consistency signal between language and visual memory without requiring external OOD annotations.
- The approach integrates into existing pipelines as a drop-in module, outperforming prompt-based baselines on ABSTAINEQA and the newly introduced SPACEREJECT benchmark for spatial localization.

---

[LOPAL: Local Performance-Aware Active Learning from Imperfect Demonstrations](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16888)

- LOPAL: introduces a framework that leverages local demonstration quality information to synthesize superior robot behaviors from imperfect human demonstrations.
- The approach utilizes a GMM to encode local performance metrics, enabling GMR to generate trajectories that selectively follow high-quality segments while interpolating missing data.
- An active data acquisition component employs shared autonomy and speed modulation to guide users in providing targeted corrections, effectively reducing both task performance errors and human teaching effort.

---

[Human-on-the-Bridge: Scalable Evaluation for AI Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16871)

- HOB (Human-on-the-Bridge): introduces a scalable evaluation paradigm for AI agents that shifts human expertise upstream to curate reusable evaluation intelligence, including Red-Team Traps, Juror Personas, Scoring Guidelines, Audit Rules, and Fallback Policies.
- The ProofAgent Harness executes multi-turn adversarial trials using a Harness LLM to produce evidence-linked reports, enabling cost-efficient asymmetric evaluation where smaller models surface objective failures.
- This paradigm addresses procedural and behavioral failures in agents by linking scores to trace-level evidence, ensuring evaluation continuity through robust fallback handling.

---

[Video-Based Optimal Transport for Feedback-Efficient Offline Preference-Based Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16856)

- VOTP (Video-based Optimal Transport Preference): introduces a semi-supervised framework that leverages optimal transport over ViFM latent spaces to generate high-fidelity pseudo-labels for unlabeled trajectory segments.
- The framework utilizes ViFMs to embed visual segments into a structured latent space, enabling the propagation of human preferences from a small set of labeled data to large amounts of unlabeled data.
- By replacing manual reward engineering with OT-based pseudo-labeling, the approach significantly improves feedback efficiency and robustness in offline RL tasks across locomotion and manipulation benchmarks.

---

[Evolution &amp; Foundation: AI Shares Creative Control](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16849)

- EvolF: introduces a human-machine collaborative framework that integrates FormGrow, Mutator, and Gemini to automate aesthetic curation in 3D evolutionary art.
- The system utilizes Gemini as an AI agent to perform binary tournament selections based on semantic goals, replacing traditional manual curation with automated aesthetic reasoning.
- By leveraging chain-of-thought prompting and JSON-constrained output, the framework generates transparent audit trails of the AI's decision-making process throughout the evolutionary experiment.

---

[Towards LLM Accelerated Rapid Reviews for Software Tool Discovery - Case for Log Anomaly Detection](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16839)

- LLM Accelerated Rapid Review Pipeline: introduces an automated methodology for identifying and executing software tools from academic literature using LLMs for screening and repository management.
- The framework utilizes LLM Screening to filter relevant papers, followed by regex-based Tool Link Extraction and an agentic LLM Repository Runner to automate the installation and testing of identified software tools.
- The pipeline significantly reduces human effort in rapid reviews by automating the screening of 3233 papers and the execution of 83 identified tools within an isolated Virtual Environment.

---

[CacheWise: Understanding Workloads and Optimizing KVCache Management for Efficiently Serving LLM Coding Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16824)

- CacheWise: introduces an agent-aware KVCache management layer that optimizes coding agent workloads by combining prefix-aware request scheduling with predictive KVCache eviction.
- The framework utilizes session metadata and tool call information to predict future KVCache reuse, effectively reducing eviction overhead and improving overall token goodput.
- By prioritizing requests with high prefix overlap and evicting blocks based on predicted reuse likelihood, the system significantly reduces session completion times for long-running, closed-loop coding agent sessions.

---

[How Much Can We Trust LLM Search Agents? Measuring Endorsement Vulnerability to Web Content Manipulation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16821)

- SearchGEO: introduces a controlled evaluation framework for measuring endorsement corruption in LLM-based search agents by combining a web-evidence manipulation pipeline, a five-mode attack taxonomy, and multiple output-level metrics.
- The framework evaluates 13 LLM backends across 308 cases each, revealing that vulnerability patterns vary significantly by model choice and that trust-signal attacks are the dominant surface for endorsement corruption.
- The study demonstrates that aggregated attack success rates often understate attacker influence, as failed attacks frequently exhibit silent semantic shifts and robust models may suffer from over-refusal in legitimate scenarios.

---

[Verification of Stochastic Dominance Envy-Freeness in Time Proportional to Input Size](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16816)

- SD-EF/SD-EF1 Verification Algorithm: introduces a time-optimal verification method for Stochastic Dominance Envy-Freeness and its relaxation, SD-EF1, using gets-array, dirty-bit-array, count-array, and prefix-dominance-checker.
- The algorithm achieves linear time complexity O(nm) by replacing pairwise agent comparisons with a single-pass iteration over each agent's preference list.
- Lazy initialization with version arrays is employed to eliminate initialization overhead, ensuring the verification process remains proportional to the input size.

---

[GIST-CMTF: Goal-State Inference for Causal Minimal Tool Filtering in LLM Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16813)

- GIST-CMTF: introduces a goal-state inference layer that predicts candidate symbolic goals and uses clarification as a causal action to prevent wrong-goal execution in LLM agents.
- The framework integrates an ambiguity detector to determine if a user request is sufficiently specified before triggering downstream Causal Minimal Tool Filtering (CMTF).
- By treating clarification as a first-class causal action within the precondition-effect framework, GIST-CMTF significantly reduces wrong-goal execution while maintaining minimal tool exposure.

---

[Scaling LLM Reasoning from Minimal Labels: A Semi-Supervised Framework with a Lightweight Verifier](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16811)

- Semi-supervised reasoning framework: introduces a method that scales LLM reasoning by training a lightweight Mcls on minimal labeled data to filter high-confidence reasoning traces from large unlabeled pools for fine-tuning Mgen.
- The framework utilizes an entropy-based confidence filter H(pj) to select reliable pseudo-labeled samples, effectively decoupling confidence estimation from the generation process.
- Experimental results on math and visual reasoning tasks demonstrate that this approach achieves performance comparable to using 10–15× more labeled data by treating reasoning verification as a data-efficient proxy for generation.

---

[LabOSBench: Benchmarking Computer Use Agents Towards Scientific Instrument Control](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16802)

- LabOSBench: introduces a lightweight, web-based benchmark for evaluating multimodal GUI agents on scientific instrument simulators, utilizing a Multimodal GUI Agent, Coordinator, Browser-backed simulator, Playwright Driver, Task manager, Setup interpreter, Postprocess, Getter, Metrics, and Execution platform.
- The framework evaluates agents across 96 subtasks spanning eight scientific instruments, requiring precise visual grounding, feedback-driven parameter tuning, and long-horizon procedural reasoning.
- Experimental results demonstrate that while current LLMs and agentic frameworks can complete structured subtasks, they face significant challenges with feedback-intensive operations and error accumulation in end-to-end scientific workflows.

---

[OpenClaw-Skill: Collective Skill Tree Search for Agentic Large Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16774)

- CSTS (Collective Skill Tree Search): introduces a framework that leverages collective intelligence from multiple LLMs to iteratively construct structured, diverse, and generalizable trees of skills for agentic tasks.
- The framework utilizes CSN-Gen to generate candidate skills and CSN-Assess to filter them based on collective quality and transferability, ensuring robust procedural guidance for LLMs.
- CSRL (Collective Skill Reinforcement Learning) further optimizes the policy by performing group-relative advantage updates over skill-conditioned rollouts, significantly enhancing long-horizon planning and tool use capabilities.

---

[Skill-to-LoRA: From Using Skills to Learning Behaviors for Token-Efficient LLM Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16769)

- S2L (Skill-to-LoRA): introduces a behavior-centric representation method that replaces runtime procedural text with dynamically loaded, skill-specific LoRA adapters to improve token efficiency.
- The framework utilizes a skill-based self-distillation pipeline to synthesize behavioral demonstrations from procedural documents, which are then distilled into lightweight LoRA parameters.
- By shifting from repeated prompt injection to dynamic adapter activation, the system reduces runtime context overhead while maintaining or improving task performance on software engineering benchmarks.

---

[Fair Division by Contribution: A Shapley Value Perspective](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16743)

- SVF: introduces a fairness framework for resource allocation without monetary transfers that uses the Shapley value as a normative benchmark to account for agents' heterogeneous contributions to social welfare.
- The paper establishes tight worst-case approximation bounds for SVF, specifically O(ln n) for general concave valuations and O(ln D) for capped concave valuations, where D is the maximum aggregate demand.
- The research demonstrates that near-optimal SVF allocations can be computed in polynomial time using convex optimization and sampling, with further refinements for linear valuations based on agent types and value dispersion.

---

[Pride and Prejudice: Toward an Information-Theoretic Framework for Mutually Communicative Driver Behavior Modeling](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16735)

- C-MIRL: introduces an information-theoretic framework for implicit mutual communication in lane-change interactions by modeling epistemic uncertainty through a level-k Bayesian persuasion game with pride, prejudice, and inquiry rewards.
- The framework utilizes virtual features to enable proactive signaling and adaptive communication affordances, allowing the ego vehicle to balance self-interest with the need to listen and express intent.
- The model is calibrated using a hierarchical multi-agent inverse reinforcement learning algorithm and validated through naturalistic driving data and Driver-In-the-Loop experiments, demonstrating improved human-likeness and safety in mixed-autonomy traffic.

---

[A First-Principles Derivation of LLM Policy Optimization: From Expected Reward to GRPO and Its Structural Extensions](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16733)

- GRPO (Group Relative Policy Optimization): introduces a unified diagnostic framework for LLM policy optimization by decomposing the objective into trajectory-side and reward-side axes.
- The framework maps existing RL methods as interventions on these two axes to address specific failure modes, including trajectory-side instabilities and reward-side credit assignment issues.
- It further extends this diagnostic map to structural extensions such as Agentic RL and the GRPO-OPD hybrid, identifying coupled failures that require joint design of both axes.

---

[AgentFairBench: Do LLM Agents Discriminate When They Act?](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16723)

- AgentFairBench: introduces a multi-domain, action-level fairness benchmark that operationalizes the BCF (Bias Conduction Framework) to measure demographic disparity in LLM agents across hiring, lending, and medical triage domains.
- The framework utilizes a scaffold-stratified design (C0–C4) to test for super-additivity and employs an arity-matched noise floor to prevent the misinterpretation of sampling noise as demographic bias.
- The benchmark includes an anti-gaming protocol with held-out private test splits and contamination canaries to ensure that reported disparity estimates reflect genuine model behavior rather than memorization.

---

[Misinformation Propagation in Benign Multi-Agent Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16710)

- MAD (Multi-Agent Debate): introduces a controlled analysis of how contextual misinformation affects benign multi-agent systems across reasoning, knowledge, and ethical alignment tasks.
- The framework utilizes the MINT dataset to inject intent-based misinformation into LLM agents, evaluating system robustness through varying group compositions and decision-making protocols.
- Results indicate that while misinformation degrades performance, multi-agent debate provides a more resilient reasoning interface than single-agent setups, particularly when uninformed agents form a majority.

---

[User as Code: Executable Memory for Personalized Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16707)

- UaC (User as Code): introduces a memory paradigm where an agent's model of a user is a living software project, utilizing a two-phase pipeline of Thinking LLM, Extraction Agent, Structuring LLM, Python Interpreter, Append-only Fact List, Typed Python Dataclasses, ChromaDB Archive, Constraint Runner, Manifest, and Active Alerts to enable deterministic reasoning and proactive service.
- The architecture separates memory into an append-only fact extraction phase and a periodic structuring phase, allowing the agent to maintain a persistent, executable state that supports complex analytical queries and state-triggered alerts.
- By representing user memory as typed Python objects, the framework enables deterministic verification and proactive service, outperforming traditional retrieval-based memory systems on aggregate reasoning and safety-critical tasks.

---

[Multimodal Evaluator Preference Collapse: Cross-Modal Contagion in Self-Evolving Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16682)

- MM-EPC: introduces a framework to quantify how evaluator preferences in a feedback loop cause systematic strategy collapse and cross-modal contagion in LLMs.
- The study utilizes TTRL to demonstrate that cross-model evaluation significantly amplifies preference collapse compared to self-evaluation, with visual tasks exhibiting higher susceptibility.
- The research establishes that contagion is evaluator-conditional and can be mitigated through self-evaluation, providing a formal Contagion Matrix to track bias transfer across modalities.

---

[The Integrator Advantage: Controlled Agentic AI for Small and Medium-Sized Companies](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16649)

- AIF-SMC: introduces a structured, human-centered framework for integrating agentic AI into business processes by defining seven technical layers and five core components for controlled autonomy.
- The framework emphasizes the "integrator" role, which bridges technical system interfaces with organizational governance, security, and workforce enablement to ensure safe and reliable AI deployment.
- It provides practical tools including a use-case radar, an autonomy ladder, and a 90-day implementation plan to help SMCs transition from experimental pilots to governed, productive agentic workflows.

---

[CoffeeBench: Benchmarking Long-Horizon LLM Agents in Heterogeneous Multi-Agent Economies](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16613)

- CoffeeBench: introduces a multi-agent benchmark for evaluating LLM agents in a simulated coffee supply chain, utilizing ReAct framework, Marketplace, Production, Financial, Demand, Communication, and Time control components.
- The framework simulates heterogeneous firms—farmers, roasters, and retailers—that must autonomously manage inventory, pricing, and inter-agent transactions to maximize cumulative net income over a 90-day horizon.
- Experimental results demonstrate that while most LLMs outperform passive baselines, some models exhibit an idle-drift failure mode, highlighting the need for sustained strategic planning and active communication in long-horizon economic tasks.

---

[ARB4WM: An Adversarial Robustness Benchmark for World Models in Continuous Control](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16605)

- ARB4WM: introduces a unified evaluation framework for assessing the adversarial robustness of world-model agents in continuous control across five component-level attack objectives.
- The framework evaluates Dreamer-style agents by targeting policy distributions, value estimation, latent representations, and RSSM dynamics consistency using various temporal exposure protocols.
- Results demonstrate that adversarial perturbations targeting internal world-model components are highly effective, highlighting the necessity of evaluating the full latent decision-making pipeline for safety-critical applications.

---

[SING: Synthetic Intention Graph for Scalable Active Tool Discovery in LLM Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16591)

- SING: introduces an intention-aware active tool discovery framework that builds an intention–tool graph to link user intentions with tool capabilities for scalable retrieval in large agentic ecosystems.
- The framework utilizes a dynamic ReAct-style process to update tool discovery based on evolving task states and subgoals, effectively reducing context-heavy schema injection.
- By modeling structural relations between intentions, servers, and tools, SING enables agents to discover collaborative tool structures and improves downstream task success rates while significantly reducing schema exposure.

---

[Infant Spontaneous Movement Noise Improves Exploration in Deep RL](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16590)

- Infant-inspired exploration framework: introduces a developmentally scheduled action noise mechanism that mimics the increasing temporal auto-correlation observed in infant spontaneous movements to enhance exploration in deep RL.
- The framework utilizes OpenPose to extract end-effector velocities from infant videos, which are then analyzed via PSD to derive a linear developmental schedule for the noise exponent β.
- By integrating this scheduled colored noise into TD3 and SAC agents, the approach facilitates a curriculum of exploration that transitions from stochastic to structured behaviors, significantly improving learning efficiency across various continuous control tasks.

---

[DRIFT: Risk-Constrained Diffusion with Imitation Priors for Mixed-Autonomy Traffic Generation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16589)

- DRIFT: introduces a closed-loop traffic generation framework that unifies Module A, Module B, and Module C to produce executable trajectories in mixed-autonomy environments.
- The framework utilizes a rolling-horizon pipeline where Module B generates diverse candidates, which are then filtered for feasibility and selected by Psel based on safety and efficiency metrics.
- By integrating long-tail risk feedback from Module C, DRIFT enables robust traffic-level evaluation and adaptation to varying AV penetration rates within the Flow/SUMO environment.

---

[Can LLM Agents Infer World Models? Evidence from Agentic Automata Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16576)

- Agentic Automata Learning: introduces a controlled framework for evaluating whether LLM agents can infer hidden deterministic finite automata through interactive membership and equivalence queries.
- The framework utilizes an oracle to provide feedback on hypothesized automata, enabling systematic measurement of agentic capabilities like adaptive planning, reasoning, and memory organization.
- Experimental results demonstrate that while advanced reasoning LLMs can perform non-trivial interactive discovery, their performance degrades sharply as environment complexity increases, often failing to match the robustness of classic automata-learning algorithms.

---

[MIRAGE: Auditing Anti-Muslim Bias in Frontier LLMs Across Reasoning, Agentic, and Time-Coupled Conditions](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16562)

- MIRAGE: introduces a benchmark of 1,200 matched-pair prompts to evaluate anti-Muslim bias in LLMs across direct completion, chain-of-thought reasoning, and agentic decision-making conditions.
- The framework demonstrates that chain-of-thought reasoning amplifies bias, agentic decisions exhibit systematic asymmetry, and bias is time-coupled to retrieved news context.
- The study reveals that existing prompt-based mitigations are largely ineffective at addressing bias in agentic and reasoning-time deployment regimes.

---

[ROSA-RL: Uncertainty-Aware Roundabout Optimized Speed Advisory with Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16558)

- ROSA-RL: introduces a conflict-zone-centric occupancy prediction model that leverages a Transformer architecture to forecast traffic occupancy over a five-second horizon, providing probabilistic inputs for downstream decision-making.
- The framework integrates these probabilistic forecasts into a PPO-based RL agent, which approximates a belief-MDP to generate proactive speed advisories for vehicles in mixed traffic.
- By explicitly modeling prediction uncertainty, ROSA-RL achieves robust performance in partially observable roundabout scenarios, significantly reducing waiting times, stops, and energy consumption compared to deterministic baselines.

---

[Can LLM Coding Agents Reason About Time Series?](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16545)

- LLM-based Time Series Analysis Agents: introduces a benchmarking framework to evaluate LLMs on time series understanding tasks using three distinct setups: Mdirect, Mcode, and Mhybrid.
- The framework utilizes an Ljudge component to analyze reasoning traces and identify failure modes, revealing that while code access improves accuracy, agents often over-rely on tool outputs or misinterpret them.
- Experimental results demonstrate that Mhybrid consistently outperforms other setups, though all agents struggle with subtle reasoning nuances and methodological errors in complex time series tasks.

---

[Generated, Parallel, Scalable? A Study of Agentic AI-Generated Julia Code on Supercomputers](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16534)

- Agentic AI-Generated Julia Code Framework: introduces a systematic evaluation of LLM-based agents for generating parallel Julia code using OpenCode, a Julia-documentation MCP server, and a Sandbox environment.
- The framework utilizes LLMs to autonomously plan, generate, and refine code for HPC tasks, comparing performance across Dagger.jl, Base.Threads, and MPI.jl.
- Experimental results demonstrate that while agents reliably produce executable code for small inputs, they struggle with robustness and scalability in large-scale HPC settings due to scheduling and dependency management challenges.

---

[SkillWiki: A Living Knowledge Infrastructure for Agent Skills](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16523)

- SkillWiki: introduces a unified infrastructure for large-scale agent skill ecosystems that transforms heterogeneous knowledge materials into governed, versioned, and traceable skill assets.
- The framework integrates Knowledge Sources, Runtime Agents, Knowledge-to-Skill Production, Governance &amp; Lifecycle Management, Provenance &amp; Relationship Modeling, Self-Management Agents, and Governed Skill Ecosystem to enable a closed-loop evolution of agent capabilities.
- By employing self-management agents and Git-style governance, the system ensures that skills are continuously produced, validated, and adapted based on execution feedback and performance monitoring.

---

[Agile Fall Recovery for Quadrotors with Bidirectional Thrust via Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16513)

- RL-based Fall Recovery Framework: introduces an asymmetric actor–critic architecture that enables quadrotors to recover from arbitrary ground-resting attitudes to stable hover using only lightweight onboard sensors.
- The framework utilizes a recurrent policy with a GRU to infer latent states from noisy, partial observations, while an INDI controller manages low-level motor dynamics to ensure robust execution.
- High-fidelity simulation with domain randomization and bidirectional thrust capabilities facilitates zero-shot sim-to-real transfer for aggressive recovery maneuvers without explicit state estimation.

---

[daVinci-kernel: Co-Evolving Skill Selection, Summarization, and Utilization via RL for GPU Kernel Optimization](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16497)

- daVinci-kernel: introduces a reinforcement learning framework that couples skill discovery with skill exploitation through a dynamically evolving skill library to optimize GPU kernels.
- The framework jointly trains a Skill Selection Agent, a Policy Agent, and a Skill Summary Agent sharing a single LLM backbone to ensure optimization knowledge remains aligned with the agent's evolving capability frontier.
- Empirical results on KernelBench demonstrate that daVinci-kernel significantly outperforms prior RL-trained models by leveraging co-evolving skill-policy optimization and execution-based verification.

---

[HATS: A Human–Agent Teleoperation System for Multi-Arm Data Collection](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16491)

- HATS: introduces a human-agent teleoperation system that decouples control space by assigning primary arms to a human pilot and assistive arms to an MLLM-based agent copilot for multi-arm data collection.
- The framework integrates Visual Perception Module, LLM Planner, Collaboration Module, and Supervision Module to enable a single operator to manage four robotic arms without mode-switching.
- HATS utilizes ASR, VLM, and AnyGrasp to facilitate real-time human-in-the-loop interventions and robust task execution, achieving performance comparable to expert dual-human teams.

---

[Steering Emotional Dynamics for Art Therapy: Controllable Narrative Script Generation through Hierarchically Guided LLM Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16481)

- EC-Script: introduces a hierarchical LLM agent-based framework that generates long-form scripts with controllable affective trajectories for therapeutic applications.
- The framework utilizes Emotion-Trajectory Planning, Character-Driven Scene Generation, and Emotion-Controlled Script Writing to ensure narrative adherence to specified emotional patterns.
- By integrating VAD emotional parameters, the system achieves fine-grained control over character-level and scene-level emotional dynamics, significantly outperforming baseline methods in affective trajectory adherence.

---

[HOLO-MPPI: Multi-Scenario Motion Planning via Hierarchical Policy Optimization](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16480)

- HOLO-MPPI: introduces a hierarchical motion planning framework that decouples high-level intention learning from low-level stochastic optimal control to achieve scenario-robust performance.
- The framework utilizes a High-Level Policy to generate data-driven sampling priors for MPPI Refinement, which performs constraint-aware trajectory optimization in real time.
- By operating in an abstract action space, the system generalizes across diverse driving scenarios while maintaining smooth control and high success rates without per-scenario retuning.

---

[When Agent Automation Becomes Profitable: Quantifying and Insuring Autonomous AI Risk through Trace-Economic Underwriting](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16465)

- Trace-Economic Underwriting: introduces a framework for quantifying and insuring autonomous AI risk by mapping agent execution traces to economic loss objects at the customer-task-trace episode level.
- The framework utilizes deterministic trace-to-loss rules to enable auditable pricing, pre-loss control, and risk transfer without relying on LLMs as label judges.
- Empirical validation on synthetic portfolios and real SWE-smith traces demonstrates that trace-conditioned pricing reduces cross-subsidy while trace-aware control effectively mitigates tail risk.

---

[RHO: Your Coding Agent is Secretly a Roboticist](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16458)

- RHO: introduces a paradigm for evolving interpretable neurosymbolic multi-file robot-policy repositories by shifting the computational burden from test-time code generation to training-time reflective evolutionary optimization.
- The framework utilizes HELIX to mutate whole repositories using a tool-enabled coding agent that interacts with a REaaS to refine policies against environment feedback.
- RHO achieves state-of-the-art performance on robotics benchmarks using single-turn execution, eliminating the need for iterative LLM code-generation loops at deployment.

---

[V2P-Manip: Learning Dexterous Manipulation from Monocular Human Videos](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16436)

- V2P-Manip: introduces a hierarchical framework that transforms raw monocular human videos into high-quality dexterous manipulation trajectories through Reconstruction Module, Refinement Module, Hand Imitator, Residual Learner, and Isaac Gym Simulator.
- The framework employs a two-stage optimization process to rectify spatial misalignments and enforce physical feasibility, followed by residual reinforcement learning to bridge the gap between human demonstrations and robotic execution.
- Experimental results demonstrate that the approach achieves high success rates across multiple synthetic tasks and generalizes effectively to diverse dexterous hand embodiments.

---

[Autonomous End-to-End SOH Prediction Services for Battery Systems via Temporal-Contrastive Representation Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16434)

- TC-SOH (Temporal-Contrastive State of Health): introduces a modular, plug-and-play service architecture that utilizes a CNN Module (1D-CNN for feature extraction), a TC Module (Temporal-contrastive sequence transformer), and an Output Module (Linear layer for SOH mapping) to perform autonomous, end-to-end SOH prediction.
- The framework employs a Diagnostic Interpretation Chain (Visualization, sensitivity, redundancy, and probing analysis) to ensure model transparency and validate the efficacy of learned representations against expert descriptors.
- By leveraging temporal-contrastive learning and cross-window prediction, the architecture extracts non-linear degradation dynamics from raw operational data, significantly reducing reliance on manual feature engineering.

---

[ACCORD: Action-Conditioned Contextual Grounding for Language Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16432)

- ACCORD (Action-Conditioned Contextual Grounding): introduces a two-layer agent framework that improves task completion by ensuring every state-mutating action is grounded in objective environmental signals through a Main Agent, a Grounding Agent, and Read-only Probes.
- The framework utilizes a policy-level prompt to encourage early environment exploration and an inference-time grounding agent that intercepts write actions to re-surface trajectory evidence or probe the environment for missing facts.
- By treating the environment as a source of information rather than just a site for execution, ACCORD effectively resolves underspecified user intents and reduces grounding failures across diverse benchmarks.

---

[LecturaAgents: A Multi-Agent Framework for Adaptive Personalized AI-Assisted Learning and Embodied Teaching](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16428)

- LecturaAgents: introduces a hierarchical multi-agent framework for end-to-end adaptive personalized learning that includes planning-, research-, generation-, review- and delivery-agents.
- The framework utilizes a ProfessorAgent to lead a collaborative team of specialized agents through the entire lecture life cycle, supported by LLM Module, Agent Module, TASA Module, and Adaptive Memory Module.
- LecturaAgents employs a Teaching Action-Speech Alignment (TASA) algorithm to generate coherent, embodied teaching actions like handwriting and rough notations that are synchronized with lecture speech.

---

[Transferable Self-Evolving Playbooks for Agentic Security Auditing](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16420)

- EVOHUNT: introduces an autonomous framework that evolves reusable security audit playbooks through a continual learning loop of discovery, evaluation, and revision.
- The framework utilizes a Discovery Agent, Evaluator, and Reviser to iteratively refine a versioned Playbook Repository, enabling the transfer of procedural knowledge to weaker LLMs via a lightweight Adapter.
- By decoupling audit procedure from model weights, EVOHUNT demonstrates that disciplined security auditing can be distilled into inspectable, transferable artifacts that improve performance across diverse agent environments.

---

[PathRouter: Aligning Rewards with Retrieval Quality in Agentic Graph Retrieval-Augmented Generation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16409)

- PathRouter: introduces a path-aware training framework for agentic GraphRAG that jointly evaluates trajectories using answer correctness and evidence-path overlap to mitigate reward aliasing and search-update ambiguity.
- The framework employs route-conditioned GRPO advantage scaling to suppress shortcut reinforcement and utilizes a frozen gold-evidence teacher to provide selective token-level KL guidance for evidence-poor trajectories.
- By masking answer tokens and warming up the KL constraint, the approach ensures the agent learns faithful retrieval strategies without directly imitating final responses.

---

[Game-Theoretic Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Swarm Trajectory Planning in Low-Altitude Wireless Networks](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16386)

- CTDE-MAPPO (Centralized Training Decentralized-Execution Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization): introduces a cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning framework for swarm trajectory planning in low-altitude wireless networks, utilizing a Decentralized Actor, Centralized Critic, Shared Rollout Buffer, Congestion-Aware Observation Space, and Per-step Reward Decomposition.
- The framework models UAV trajectory planning as a cooperative stochastic congestion game, where the Centralized Critic leverages global congestion information to guide decentralized actors during training.
- Simulation results demonstrate that the approach achieves superior aggregate utility and mission success rates compared to independent learning and value-decomposition baselines by effectively managing shared resource block congestion.

---

[Looking Is Not Picking: An Attention-Segment Account of Tool-Selection Failures in LLM Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16364)

- HAA (Harness Attention Allocation): introduces a training-free, causally actionable diagnostic and intervention framework for LLM tool-selection failures by analyzing attention mass over labeled tool-definition segments.
- The framework utilizes an additive attention-logit bias to demonstrate that tool-selection failures are localized to the readout bottleneck rather than the harness input.
- A gold-free selector component leverages per-segment attention to improve tool-calling accuracy by gating decisions based on output-logprob margins.

---

[The Proxy Knows Too Much: Sealing LLM API Routers with Attested TEEs](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16358)

- AEGIS: introduces a provider-transparent API router that secures LLM interactions by confining plaintext handling to a hardware-enclave component while leaving management on an untrusted host.
- The framework utilizes a client-verified attested enclave to ensure that only authorized, unmodified requests reach the provider, effectively blocking malicious router attack classes.
- By maintaining a minimal trusted base and anchoring measurements to audited source code via a transparency log, AEGIS provides a verifiable trust boundary for LLM API routing.

---

[GraphBEV++: Multi-Modal Feature Alignment for Autonomous Driving](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16354)

- GraphBEV++: introduces a robust multi-modal fusion framework that mitigates projection-induced feature misalignment in autonomous driving through LocalAlign-v2 and GlobalAlign-v2 modules.
- The framework utilizes LocalAlign-v2 to address local geometric correspondence errors via neighborhood-aware depth features and GlobalAlign-v2 to resolve global representation-level inconsistencies using deformable or diffusion-based alignment.
- GraphBEV++ is compatible with both LSS-based and query-based BEV representations, demonstrating state-of-the-art performance in 3D object detection, occupancy prediction, and end-to-end autonomous driving tasks.

---

[AdaSTORM: Scaling LLM Reasoning on Dynamic Graphs via Adaptive Spatio-Temporal Multi-Agent Collaboration](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16328)

- AdaSTORM: introduces a multi-agent framework that reformulates large-scale dynamic graph reasoning into adaptive partitioning and collaborative reasoning stages to bypass LLM scaling bottlenecks.
- The framework utilizes a Capacity Estimator, Cost Estimator, and RL-based Adaptive Partitioner to decompose graphs into capacity-matched subregions, which are then processed by specialized Spatial Agents and Temporal Agents.
- AdaSTORM achieves state-of-the-art performance on dynamic graph benchmarks by leveraging spatio-temporal decoupling and multi-agent orchestration to scale reasoning to thousand-node networks without external tools.

---

[Gaming-Resistant Insurance Contracts for Autonomous AI Agents: Strategy-Proof Toll Mechanism Design](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16326)

- Composition Contract: introduces an incentive-compatibility layer for actuarial control of autonomous AI agents by composing Paper A clauses, Aggregate settlement, Ambiguity-reserve extension, Escalation adjudication, Type-reporting penalty schedule, and Premium schedule.
- The framework addresses a five-attack space for autonomous AI-agent insurance contracts, ensuring that operator strategies such as within-boundary splitting, post-toll safe-default selection, cross-boundary re-routing, interface-compliance gaming, and model-identity misreporting are deterred.
- By combining these components, the framework achieves joint incentive compatibility, individual rationality, and weak budget balance, effectively making truthful contract-following weakly dominant for the operator.

---

[Architectural Wisdom: A Framework for Governing Optimization in AI Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16319)

- Architectural Wisdom Framework: introduces a corrigible objective-governance layer that sits above the optimization substrate to ensure AI systems operate within defined temporal, relational, and irreversibility constraints.
- The framework utilizes a Structural Utility Transform, Moral Admissibility Interface, Arbitration and Escalation Controller, and Value Revision Channel to compute a six-coordinate wisdom tuple for governing AI objectives.
- This approach distinguishes intelligence from wisdom by requiring that AI systems explicitly account for long-term consequences, affected parties, and irreversible losses before executing tasks.

---

[RL-Index: Reinforcement Learning for Retrieval Index Reasoning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16316)

- RL-Index: introduces an agentic indexing framework that shifts reasoning from online query rewriting to offline document augmentation using reinforcement learning.
- The framework utilizes an Agentic Indexer to generate logical rationales for documents, which are optimized via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) using retrieval-based rewards.
- By encoding latent query-knowledge relationships into the index offline, the system improves retrieval effectiveness and downstream QA performance while significantly reducing online inference latency.

---

[Is Your Trajectory Displacement Safe in Long-tail?](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16313)

- FluidTest: introduces a human-aligned, safety-aware, and explainable evaluation pipeline for long-tail autonomous driving that reframes planning assessment as additional-threat detection relative to expert trajectories.
- The framework utilizes a multi-agent system comprising a Prosecutor-agent, Detector-agent, and Judge-agent to perform neuro-symbolic verification of planner trajectories against a comprehensive Threat-Library.
- By employing a pairwise WebUI for human annotation and evidence-grounded Decision-Graphs, the pipeline provides a stable, planner-independent metric called No Additional Threat Rate (NATR) that outperforms traditional aggregate displacement metrics.

---

[State-Grounded Multi-Agent Synthetic Data Generation for Tool-Augmented LLMs](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16307)

- STATEGEN: introduces a synthetic data generation platform that orchestrates a four-role LLM loop supported by an authoritative state manager to produce tool-grounded, multi-turn conversational data.
- The framework utilizes a state-grounded tool simulation paradigm where a central world state object acts as the single source of truth to eliminate tool-call hallucinations.
- STATEGEN supports hierarchical multi-agent generation by declaring sub-agents as tools that share a single authoritative state object, ensuring cross-agent consistency during data generation.

---

[VisualClaw: A Real-Time, Personalized Agent for the Physical World](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16295)

- VisualClaw: introduces a self-evolving multimodal agent that utilizes Cascade Gate G, Skill Bank S, Memory Store Mv, LLM Evolver E, Hybrid Encoding, Hot/Cold Skill Injection, and Memory-Augmented Meta-Evolution to enable efficient, personalized, and adaptive deployment for physical world tasks.
- The framework employs a three-timescale system where an edge-side cascade filters streaming video, while a memory-augmented evolver updates the skill bank offline to improve performance without modifying VLM weights.
- VisualClaw significantly reduces API costs by filtering redundant frames and optimizes agent performance through continuous, failure-driven evolution of its skill and memory banks.

---

[Distributed General-Purpose Agent Networks: Architecture, Key Mechanisms, and Prototypes](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17368)

- Distributed General-Purpose Agent Networks: introduces a layered architecture for decentralized agent collaboration, utilizing a protocol adaptation layer to bridge task semantics with peer-to-peer network operations.
- The framework integrates collaborator discovery, verifiable identity governance via BAID, and automated mechanism design using a Stackelberg-style loop between mechanism-generation and strategy-exploration agents.
- The system employs MG-EigenTrust for dynamic, cross-domain reputation management and uses semantic-gradient feedback to iteratively refine cooperation rules against adversarial strategies.

---

[DriveJudge: Rethinking Autonomous Driving Evaluation with Vision-Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17362)

- DriveJudge: introduces a context-aware evaluation agent that combines VLM reasoning with rule-grounded evaluation to selectively invoke physically-grounded deterministic rule functions.
- The framework utilizes a VLM to interpret environmental context and predict a gating vector, which determines the relevance of specific rule-based metrics for a given driving state.
- DriveJudge employs a two-stage post-training pipeline, combining supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning, to improve contextual reasoning and rule invocation accuracy.

---

[MemTrace: Probing What Final Accuracy Misses in Long-Term Memory](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17328)

- MemTrace: introduces a knowledge-point benchmark that evaluates LLM long-term memory by probing facts across controlled dimensions of memory age, question type, and evidence condition.
- The framework identifies that long-term memory failures are primarily evidence-use bottlenecks rather than retrieval limitations, as systems often fail to utilize reachable evidence.
- MemTrace evaluates 13 memory-system configurations, revealing that pooled accuracy metrics hide critical failures in trajectory tracking and conflict resolution.

---

[ProCUA-SFT Technical Report](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17321)

- ProCUA-SFT: introduces a large-scale, synthetic dataset of 3.1M step-level samples for training computer-use agents, utilizing Kimi-K2.5 as a unified goal generator, precondition judge, and trajectory executor.
- The pipeline ensures task feasibility by seeding desktops with real-world content from SpreadsheetBench and Zenodo10K, while employing in-loop binary precondition verification to eliminate infeasible goals.
- By applying step-prefix expansion to synthetic trajectories, the framework enables LLMs to learn from growing-context layouts, significantly improving performance on complex, multi-application desktop tasks.

---

[PDAGENT-BENCH: Characterizing, Grounding, and Architecting LLM Agents for VLSI Physical Design](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17253)

- PDAgent: introduces a unified, human-aligned agentic workflow framework that enables closed-loop evaluation of holistic physical design in realistic EDA environments using Planner Agent, Worker Agent, Debugger Agent, Optimizer Agent, Analyzer Agent, MetricsDB, Skill Packs, and DesignContext.
- The framework utilizes a multi-agent architecture where specialized agents coordinate to perform iterative design refinement, leveraging expert-curated skill libraries to overcome limitations in tool-specific script generation.
- PDAGENT-BENCH provides a standardized, multi-dimensional benchmark suite of 353 curated problems to evaluate LLM/VLM-based agents across foundational knowledge, report comprehension, root-cause analysis, script generation, and full-flow implementation.

---

[Rethinking Groups in Critic-Free RLVR](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17250)

- NTF: introduces a strategy for stable single-rollout RL by masking high-probability tokens in negative trajectories to prevent harmful updates on shared supporting tokens.
- The framework enables critic-free and group-free training by concentrating negative updates on lower-probability tokens, thereby preserving essential reasoning patterns and structural components.
- Empirical results demonstrate that this approach achieves competitive or superior performance compared to group-based RL methods across mathematical reasoning and agentic tasks.

---

[GeoDisaster: Benchmarking Orchestrated Agents for Operational Disaster Geo-Intelligence](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17246)

- GeoDisaster: introduces an operational geospatial disaster reasoning benchmark and an orchestrated multi-agent framework that utilizes AORC, AGA, AVRA, APA, Execution State, and Execution Contracts to perform evidence-grounded disaster analysis.
- The framework employs Role-Contract Expectation Alignment (RCEA), which combines failure-aware supervised fine-tuning with contract-grounded reinforcement learning to optimize agent behavior against dense, step-level contract obligations.
- Experiments demonstrate that the orchestrated multi-agent system significantly outperforms monolithic LLMs in tool-use fidelity, constraint satisfaction, and grounded decision-making across diverse disaster-oriented geospatial tasks.

---

[Rift: A Conflict Signature for Deception in Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17229)

- RIFT (Residual-rank Identification of Falsehoods and Truths): introduces a label-free method to identify deceptive LLM responses by detecting a measurable conflict signature in hidden states, characterized by higher Residual Rank compared to honest or naive-liar passes.
- The framework utilizes a Conflict Score to distinguish between knowledge conflict and simple incorrectness, demonstrating robustness across various model families, scales, and deception types including strategic and concealed lies.
- RIFT employs Relative Representations and Linear Probes to establish a domain-general and cross-family deception geometry, while noting that the identified signature is linearly readable but not linearly writable for steering purposes.

---

[Safety, Security, and Cognitive Risks in Neuro-Symbolic AI](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17223)

- NeSy: introduces a comprehensive threat model for hybrid AI architectures by identifying five distinct attack surfaces across neural perception modules, symbolic knowledge bases, reasoning engines, orchestration and agent layer, and data and context stores.
- The paper formalizes NeSy-specific security metrics, including Symbolic Integrity Violation and Cross-Layer Amplification Ratio, to quantify how neural perturbations propagate through symbolic reasoning chains.
- It further analyzes cognitive risks such as automation bias and sycophantic reinforcement, proposing interdisciplinary mitigations and a practitioner checklist aligned with NIST AI 600-1 and the EU AI Act.

---

[When Rules Learn: A Self-Evolving Agent for Legal Case Retrieval](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17220)

- Self-evolution framework: introduces a training-free, closed-loop agent-environment system that iteratively optimizes query-rewriting rules for legal case retrieval through Core LLM, Memory, Active Rules, Removed Rules, Experimental History, Action Decision, Rule Generation, Rule Elimination, Experiment Planning, Environment, External Rewriting LLM, Development Dataset, and Evaluator.
- The framework enhances BM25 retrieval by enabling an LLM-based agent to autonomously generate, validate, and prune rewriting rules based on historical performance feedback without requiring gradient-based training.
- Experimental results on the LeCaRD-v2 benchmark demonstrate that the framework, when powered by a high-capacity Core LLM, consistently outperforms non-evolutionary baselines by effectively leveraging historical experimental data for rule refinement.

---

[Intermittent Strategic Cooperation of Two Selfish Agents on Graphs](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17216)

- IC2PP (Intermittent Strategic Cooperation-Based Two-Agent Path Planning): introduces a game-theoretic framework for two self-interested agents navigating graphs where intermittent, time-constrained cooperation can reduce travel delays.
- The framework characterizes Pure Nash Equilibria (PNE) through a rigid structure consisting of a joining segment, a continuous cooperation segment, and a departure segment.
- The authors provide polynomial-time algorithms to enumerate non-dominated equilibrium strategies and evaluate coordination mechanisms based on bargaining-theoretic concepts.

---

[Beyond Parallel Sampling: Diverse Query Initialization for Agentic Search](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17209)

- DivInit: introduces a training-free intervention for agentic search that mitigates anchor collapse by generating a diverse pool of initial queries via an LLM-based query generator and selecting a subset using an MMR selector.
- The framework utilizes parallel search agents to explore distinct retrieval paths, effectively preventing the redundant evidence gathering observed in standard parallel sampling.
- By diversifying the first-turn query distribution, DivInit consistently improves performance across multiple LLMs and benchmarks without requiring additional training or complex aggregation logic.

---

[Trust-Aware Multi-Agent Traceability: Confidence-Calibrated Knowledge Graphs for Consistent Software Artifact Management](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17203)

- Trust-Aware Multi-Agent Traceability: introduces a coordination framework that utilizes a shared knowledge graph as an active surface for multi-agent software engineering pipelines, where agents communicate trust through calibrated confidence scores.
- The framework employs a two-stage traceability link prediction pipeline, combining embedding-based semantic retrieval with LLM-based multi-criteria analysis to produce confidence-calibrated links.
- A consistency protocol integrates confidence threshold gating, divergence detection, and conflict materialization to prevent the propagation of low-confidence decisions and ensure compliance in safety-critical software development.

---

[Verified Detection and Prevention of Concurrency Anomalies in Multi-Agent Large Language Model Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17182)

- Consistency Lattice for Multi-Agent LLM Systems: introduces a mechanically verified consistency hierarchy for multi-agent LLM systems, formalizing five concurrency anomalies and providing verified runtime strategies to prevent them.
- The framework models multi-agent systems as agents interacting with shared memory and tool registries, using TLA+ and Verus to verify anomaly detectors and runtime safety properties.
- The research demonstrates that concurrency anomalies in multi-agent LLMs are structurally similar to classical database isolation failures, but require new prevention mechanisms due to unbounded neural-inference latency.

---

[From Parasocial Scripts to Dyadic Persistence in Autonomous AI-Agent Communities](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17174)

- Theory-Guided Annotation and Testing Framework: introduces a methodology to identify and analyze parasocial interaction (PSI) and relationship (PSR) cues in autonomous AI-agent discourse using Data, Theoretical Lens, Cue Identification, Annotation Method, Hypothesis Testing, Key Results, Validation, and Robustness.
- The framework operationalizes parasocial theory by detecting attachment language, self-disclosure, and reciprocity bids in LLM-enabled agent interactions to bridge interaction-level scripts with repeated dyadic patterns.
- By employing grouped-context LLM annotation and rigorous nullification tests, the research demonstrates that AI agents exhibit context-responsive relational cues that align with sustained mutual recurrence.

---

[PROMPTMN: PSEUDO PROMPTING LANGUAGE](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17164)

- PromptMN (Prompt Pseudo-Prompting Domain-Specific Language): introduces a pseudo-prompting domain-specific language that annotates natural language with typed, mnemonic directives to improve the clarity, inspectability, and reusability of prompts for LLMs.
- The framework organizes directives into seven functional clusters, enabling structured intent expression across software development lifecycle scenarios without requiring users to become programmers.
- By providing a shared notation for roles, goals, constraints, and control flow, PromptMN reduces context ambiguities and facilitates more reliable human-to-AI interaction across various frontier LLMs.

---

[MemSlides: A Hierarchical Memory Driven Agent Framework for Personalized Slide Generation with Multi-turn Local Revision](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17162)

- MemSlides: introduces a hierarchical memory framework that separates persistent user profiles and reusable execution experience from session-level working memory to enable personalized slide generation and localized multi-turn revision.
- The framework utilizes a Plan-Act-Guard pipeline to perform targeted slide-level updates, reducing the need for full-deck regeneration and minimizing context pressure during multi-turn editing.
- Experimental results demonstrate that the integration of user profile memory and tool memory significantly improves persona alignment and localized modification reliability across various LLM-based agents.

---

[Shift-Left High-Level Synthesis Verification via Knowledge-Augmented LLM Agent](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17128)

- HLS-LeVeri: introduces a knowledge-augmented, agent-driven framework that systematically validates functional consistency between golden C specifications and HLS-oriented C implementations prior to synthesis.
- The framework utilizes a Dual-Tier Consistency Checking mechanism, integrating LLM, KLEE, and gcov to enforce static structural isomorphism and dynamic behavioral equivalence through a closed-loop verification agent.
- A heterogeneous HLS Verification Knowledge Graph provides topology-aware reasoning priors to the agent, enabling iterative refinement of testbenches to achieve high structural coverage and strict cross-domain consistency.

---

[An Evaluation of Data Leakage Risks in Tool-Using LLM Agents in Realistic Scenarios](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.17114)

- Agentic Data Leakage Evaluation Framework: introduces a joint evaluation methodology to assess data leakage risks in LLM agents during non-adversarial, multi-step workflows using ReAct Agent Scaffold, User LLM, MCP-based Tool Environment, LLM-judge, and Task-specific Rubrics.
- The framework evaluates five distinct data leakage risk categories—data awareness, audience awareness, policy compliance, data minimization, and access-boundary awareness—by analyzing full agent execution trajectories rather than final outputs.
- Experimental results demonstrate a significant gap between task completion capability and data-handling safety, highlighting that high performance in routine tasks does not guarantee adherence to privacy and security boundaries.

---

[HiMPO: Hindsight-Informed Memory Policy Optimization for Less-Entangled Credit in Long-Horizon Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16285)

- HiMPO: introduces a framework for assigning less-entangled credit to memory-writing actions in long-horizon agents by utilizing Local Counterfactual Utility, Hindsight Relevance, a Sign-consistent Gate, a Memory-success Mask, Backward Smoothing, and Token-level Policy Optimization.
- The framework addresses causally entangled credit assignment by decoupling memory-specific rewards from trajectory-level outcomes using a hindsight-gated retrospective filter.
- Experimental results demonstrate that HiMPO improves task performance and attribution fidelity in long-horizon agents while maintaining compressed-context efficiency.

---

[SpecAlign: Efficient Specification-Grounded Alignment of Large Language Models via Synthetic Data](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16276)

- SpecAlign: introduces a framework that synthesizes alignment data directly from provider-authored specification documents to operationalize them as training signals.
- The framework utilizes Rule Annotation, Specification Generation, and Multi-Agent Adversarial Data Synthesis, which includes planner-, attacker-, defender-, and judge-agents to generate fine-grained, boundary-aware preference pairs.
- By grounding data synthesis in explicit model specifications, SpecAlign enables rapid adaptation of LLMs to evolving policy requirements without manual data curation.

---

[GraphWorld: Long-Horizon Planning with World Models for End-to-End Autonomous Driving](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16274)

- GraphWorld: introduces an E2E-AD framework that enhances long-horizon planning by utilizing an Image Encoder, ECIG, WSCP, Recurrent World Encoder, Flow-Matching Module, Importance Reweighting Network, and Decoding Heads to model latent world states.
- The framework employs an Ego-Centric Interaction Graph to capture critical spatial dependencies and a World-State-Conditioned Planning module to guide trajectory generation through interaction-aware latent representations.
- By replacing explicit multi-step rollouts with a continuous-time Flow-Matching approach, the model achieves stable, long-horizon planning while maintaining high computational efficiency for real-time autonomous driving.

---

[UXBench: Measuring the Actionability of LLM-Generated UX Critiques](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16262)

- UXBench: introduces a benchmark for evaluating LLMs as interaction-grounded UX judges by measuring the actionability of their critiques through a controlled repair-and-score pipeline.
- The framework utilizes local-first web fixtures and coverage-gated browser exploration to ensure that LLMs ground their usability findings in concrete interaction evidence rather than static impressions.
- Evaluation results across eight frontier LLMs demonstrate that UX judging is a multi-dimensional capability where models exhibit distinct rubric-level repair signatures and varying levels of fixture-level reliability.

---

[Evolutionary Bilevel Reward Shaping for Generalization in Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16236)

- GERS: introduces a bilevel optimization framework that improves RL generalization by using CMA-ES to optimize reward shaping parameters based on scalar validation feedback.
- The framework utilizes PPO for lower-level policy training and CMA-ES for upper-level reward function optimization, effectively bridging the generalization gap without requiring trajectory access from validation environments.
- Empirical results on MuJoCo continuous control tasks demonstrate that GERS enhances generalization performance under restricted data access constraints, performing competitively with domain randomization.

---

[From Tokens to Regions: CUDA-Sensitive Instruction Tuning for GPU Kernel Generation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2606.16231)

- CuSeT (CUDA-Sensitive Instruction Tuning): introduces a post-training method that improves LLM-based GPU kernel generation by explicitly modeling CUDA sensitivity at both token and region levels using Confidence Mask, CUDA Mask, Hybrid Mask, Sliding Window, and Region-aware Sample Reweighting.
- The framework enhances supervision for execution-critical code by combining adaptive token-level masking to isolate informative signals and region-aware reweighting to prioritize challenging structural patterns.
- Experimental results demonstrate that CuSeT significantly improves functional correctness across multiple LLM families while maintaining high compilation success and reducing inference costs compared to standard SFT and existing baselines.

---
