- The framework utilizes a multi-agent collaborative workflow to generate high-quality, confidence-annotated demonstrations, which are then distilled into a lightweight model using a confidence-aware fine-tuning strategy and RAG.
- The dual-head architecture enables joint prediction of decision probabilities and textual rationales, facilitating efficient on-board deployment while maintaining state-of-the-art performance in complex driving scenarios.

---

[Evo-Attacker: Memory-Augmented Reinforcement Learning for Long-Horizon Tool Attacks on LLM-MAS](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25389)

- Evo-Attacker: introduces a memory-augmented reinforcement learning framework that constructs a dynamic Attack Memory and employs a deliberative reasoning policy to retrieve adversarial patterns and strategize interventions.
- The framework utilizes a Memory Retriever and Return Modifier to execute context-aware attacks, which are optimized via Attack-Flow GRPO to resolve long-horizon credit assignment challenges.
- Extensive experiments demonstrate that Evo-Attacker consistently outperforms existing baselines by generalizing across diverse LLM-MAS architectures, task domains, and tool schemas.

---

[AuthTrace: Diagnosing Evidence Construction in Thematically Dense Single-Author Corpora](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25382)

- AuthTrace: introduces a diagnostic benchmark for evaluating evidence construction systems on thematically dense single-author corpora using Flat RAG, Graph RAG, Memory, Event Memory, LLM-Wiki, and Long-context components.
- The framework utilizes a pack-level evaluation protocol measuring Evidence Recall, Evidence Precision, and Answer Correctness to diagnose paradigm-specific failure modes across varying evidence fan-in levels.
- Experimental results demonstrate that evidence recall is the primary bottleneck for answer quality, with different paradigms exhibiting distinct structural collapse patterns as the required evidence fan-in increases.

---

[EfficientGraph-RAG: Structured Retrieval-State Management for Cross-Task Retrieval-Augmented Generation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25379)

- EfficientGraph-RAG: introduces a structured retrieval-state management framework that decouples retrieval-side state updates from final reasoning to improve efficiency and accuracy.
- The framework utilizes TAM for hierarchical evidence organization, MARS for iterative multi-agent state updates, and SMP for controlled memory sharing across agents and queries.
- By offloading retrieval, navigation, and verification to small-model agents and reserving the LLM for final synthesis, the system significantly reduces token usage while maintaining high performance across diverse retrieval tasks.

---

[KYA: A Framework-Agnostic Trust Layer for Autonomous Systems with Verifiable Provenance and Hierarchical Policy Composition](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25376)

- KYA (Know Your Agents): introduces a framework-agnostic trust and governance layer for autonomous systems, utilizing kya (core trust and governance), kya_hooks (in-process agent framework shim), kya_otlp_bridge (OpenTelemetry span ingestion), kya_redteam (out-of-band adversarial testing), HMAC evidence chain (cryptographically verifiable audit log), KYP (unified trust scoring for users/agents/services), four-gate pipeline (signed policy update verification), and only-tighten algebra (hierarchical policy override enforcement) to provide verifiable provenance and hierarchical policy composition.
- The framework provides a unified principal-trust taxonomy (KYP) and a four-gate inbound apply pipeline to ensure secure, operator-approved policy distribution across diverse agent frameworks.
- KYA maintains system integrity through an HMAC-chained evidence log and an only-tighten composition algebra that prevents unauthorized policy loosening while supporting multi-tenant governance.

---

[FOUND-IT: Foundation-model-first Task-driven 3D Scene Graphs with Granularity on Demand](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25371)

- FOUND-IT: introduces a real-time, open-set, hierarchical 3D scene graph construction system that dynamically adjusts granularity based on task-specific queries using GFM, Visual Memory, Cached Memory, Places Layer, Ground Segmentation Head, Regions Layer, and an LLM Agent.
- The framework leverages uncalibrated monocular video input to build task-driven representations, deferring object formation until query time to maintain efficiency and flexibility.
- By integrating a ground segmentation head into the GFM and utilizing an LLM agent for incremental scene graph construction, the system achieves high performance in both indoor and outdoor environments without requiring predefined task lists.

---

[Prior Policy Guided Dual-Agent Coordinated Manipulation Planning of Spacecraft-Manipulator System](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25362)

- DACMP: introduces a decoupled dual-agent architecture that isolates manipulator control from base spacecraft stabilization to mitigate dynamic coupling interference.
- The framework utilizes a Timestep-level Expert Switching Guidance (TESG) mechanism to integrate prior knowledge from classical controllers, ensuring stable exploration and preventing the agents from learning mere residual compensation.
- Extensive experiments demonstrate that the DACMP framework achieves superior task success rates and control precision compared to single-agent DRL baselines under various environmental disturbances and system constraints.

---

[Towards Reliable Fetal Ultrasound Interpretation with Multi-Agent Collaboration](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25357)

- FetUSAgents: introduces a tool-augmented multi-agent system for comprehensive fetal ultrasound interpretation that orchestrates task-specific visual tools via collaborative LLM agents.
- The framework utilizes a Dual-Path Evidence Arbitration (DPEA) mechanism to integrate LLM-driven deliberative reasoning from multiple Voter Agents with structured computational evidence from specialized tools.
- A retrieval-enhanced Evidence bank consolidates heterogeneous intermediate findings to promote traceable, clinically grounded conclusions and reduce hallucinations in report generation.

---

[CausalFlow: Causal Attribution and Counterfactual Repair for LLM Agent Failures](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25338)

- CausalFlow: introduces an interventional framework that converts failed LLM agent execution traces into minimal counterfactual repairs and reusable supervision.
- The framework models execution as sequential chains of dependent steps, using Causal Responsibility Scores to identify and repair failure-inducing decisions through targeted interventions.
- CausalFlow improves agent reliability and provides structured contrastive pairs for offline preference optimization without requiring manual annotation of intermediate steps.

---

[Recursive Multi-Agent Trading System: Iterative Optimized Portfolio Strategy Under Geopolitical Uncertainty](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25311)

- RMATS: introduces a hierarchical multi-agent framework for iterative portfolio optimization that utilizes a Manager Agent, Sentiment Agent, Report Agent, Analysis Agent, and Risk Agent to achieve convergence-guaranteed signal refinement under geopolitical uncertainty.
- The system employs a recursive coordination protocol where agents exchange typed messages to iteratively adjust portfolio weights until the aggregate change falls below a defined threshold.
- Empirical results demonstrate that RMATS provides superior downside protection and lower maximum drawdown compared to traditional MVO and single-agent baselines during geopolitical stress events.

---

[Tool-Call Dependency Structure is Linearly Decodable in LLM Agent Residual Streams](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25310)

- Tool-Call Dependency Structure is Linearly Decodable in LLM Agent Residual Streams: introduces a structural probing methodology to decode directed acyclic graphs of tool-call dependencies from the residual streams of LLMs.
- The research demonstrates that tool-call dependency structures are linearly decodable from frozen residual streams of LLMs, with signals emerging in mid-stack layers and persisting through the final layer.
- Through activation patching and counterfactual analysis, the paper provides evidence that these dependency representations propagate through the model's internal state rather than acting as passive readouts or simple positional artifacts.

---

[Memory Architectures for Multi-Turn Text-to-SQL: A Benchmark and Empirical Study](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26394)

- ENTERPRISEMEM-BENCH: introduces a multi-turn Text-to-SQL benchmark that evaluates LLMs across five memory conditions, including Stateless, Working Memory, Episodic Memory, Semantic Memory, and a Context Resolution Layer.
- The framework utilizes a condition-aware SQL agent that performs parallel context resolution and retrieval to address the universal collapse of stateless LLMs in multi-turn database interactions.
- The study demonstrates that while working memory is mandatory for multi-turn performance, additional episodic and semantic memory components provide non-monotonic returns that are highly dependent on the specific model and dataset.

---

[RICE-PO: Turning Retrieval Interactions into Credit Signals for Reasoning Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26352)

- RICE-PO: introduces a critic-free policy optimization framework that converts multi-turn retrieval interactions into localized learning signals for reasoning agents.
- The framework utilizes uncertainty-guided anchor selection to identify critical reasoning steps and employs counterfactual branching to estimate the influence and residual stability of these steps on retrieval outcomes.
- By gating the propagation of summary-level retrieval rewards to latent reasoning spans, RICE-PO enables fine-grained credit assignment without requiring learned critics or external process reward models.

---

[MechRL: Reinforcement Learning Agents Perform Circuit Discovery for Mechanistic Interpretability](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26343)

- MechRL: introduces a reinforcement learning approach to automate circuit discovery in LLMs by treating attention head ablation as a Markov decision process.
- The framework utilizes a PPO Agent to identify causally indispensable attention heads by maximizing a Contrastive Reward Function that isolates task-specific performance from general model degradation.
- By employing a Best-of-K Planner, the system achieves high-performance transfer to unseen tasks, demonstrating that learned structural priors effectively identify single-head bottlenecks in transformer circuits.

---

[ScientistOne: Towards Human-Level Autonomous Research via Chain-of-Evidence](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26340)

- ScientistOne: introduces an end-to-end autonomous research system designed to maintain evidence chains by construction throughout literature review, solution discovery, and paper writing.
- The framework utilizes a Chain-of-Evidence (CoE) standard to ensure every claim is traceable to a grounding source, supported by a CoE Integrity Audit that performs score verification, specification violation detection, reference verification, and method-code alignment.
- ScientistOne achieves superior verifiability compared to baseline systems, demonstrating that maintaining evidence chains at claim-production time is essential for producing trustworthy autonomous research outputs.

---

[JobBench: Aligning Agent Work With Human Will](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26329)

- JobBench: introduces a human-centered benchmark for evaluating AI agents on professional workflows that experts prefer to delegate, utilizing an Agentic Workspace, Reference Files, Reasoning Challenges, Fact-anchored Chained Rubrics, Agentic Scaffolds, and an LLM-as-a-judge.
- The framework evaluates LLMs across 130 tasks spanning 35 occupations, grounding performance metrics in domain expert delegation preferences rather than purely economic value.
- JobBench employs a rigorous three-stage quality check pipeline, including automated audits and annotator reviews, to ensure that its 4,631 binary criteria are verifiable and challenging for frontier LLMs.

---

[Anchor: Mitigating Artifact Drift in Agent Benchmark Generation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26321)

- Anchor: introduces a task-generation pipeline that mitigates artifact drift by compiling instructions, environment configurations, oracle solutions, and verifiers from a single solved constraint program.
- The framework utilizes a CP-SAT solver to certify optimal solutions for parametric business workflows, ensuring deterministic consistency across all generated task artifacts.
- The authors apply this pipeline to produce ERP-Bench, a verifiable benchmark of 300 long-horizon procurement and manufacturing tasks, demonstrating that frontier LLMs struggle with business-rule adherence despite high constraint satisfaction rates.

---

[E³C: Video Generation with 3D Environmental Memory and Ego-Exo Human Pose Control](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26316)

- E³C: introduces a controllable egocentric video generation framework that unifies 3D Environmental Memory with Ego-Exo Human Pose Control to disentangle persistent scene structure from human-driven dynamics.
- The framework utilizes a semi-dense point cloud-based 3D memory augmented with per-point appearance features to maintain geometric consistency under rapid camera motion.
- E³C incorporates an ego pose encoder that converts 3D body joints and wrist poses into persistent cross-attention tokens, enabling robust motion control even when the wearer's body is partially occluded or out-of-view.

---

[Your Agents Are Aging Too: Agent Lifespan Engineering for Deployed Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26302)

- AgingBench: introduces a longitudinal benchmark framework for evaluating and diagnosing time-dependent reliability degradation in long-lived LLM agents.
- The framework organizes agent aging into four distinct mechanisms—compression, interference, revision, and maintenance—and utilizes a temporal dependency DAG to enable controlled, reproducible evaluation.
- AgingBench employs a component-aware diagnostic pipeline with counterfactual probes to attribute failures to specific stages, including Write/Compress Policy (W), Memory Store (S), Read/Retrieval Algorithms (R), and Utilization Logic (U).

---

[Sandlock: Confining AI Agent Code with Unprivileged Linux Primitives](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26298)

- Sandlock: introduces a split-enforcement architecture for unprivileged AI agent sandboxing that separates static kernel-enforced rules from runtime-dependent supervisor decisions.
- The framework utilizes Landlock (kernel-enforced static filesystem/network/IPC rules), seccomp-bpf (unconditional syscall filtering), seccomp user notification supervisor (async task handling runtime-dependent decisions), policy_fn (programmable runtime policy callback), COW backends (unprivileged filesystem write capture), and pipeline operator (stage-based capability composition) to provide low-latency, programmable confinement.
- Sandlock achieves sub-10 ms startup overhead and near-native performance by minimizing supervisor involvement through the use of static kernel-enforced policies for predictable operations.

---

[Agentic AI Workload Characteristics](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26297)

- Agentic AI Workload Characteristics: introduces an end-to-end tracing infrastructure to characterize ReAct-style agent workloads across LLM serving and tool execution perspectives.
- The study reveals that agentic workloads are decode-dominated due to effective context caching, while tool execution latency and failure-driven retries significantly impact end-to-end performance.
- The research demonstrates that agents exhibit a temporal shift from early exploration-heavy tool use to later action-oriented execution, with failed tasks often amplifying system load through repeated retries and increased context growth.

---

[Decoupled Delay Compensation: Enhancing Pre-trained MARL Policies via Learned Dynamics Filtering](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26286)

- Decoupled Delay Compensation framework: introduces a modular execution-stage state-estimation layer that replaces delayed observations with current belief-state estimates using a GRU Predictor, a Kalman Filter, a Fusion Module, and a pre-trained MARL Policy.
- The framework functions as a plug-in middleware that enables pre-trained MARL policies to maintain robustness against stochastic communication delays and packet loss without requiring policy retraining.
- By utilizing a learned GRU-based world model and recursive Kalman updates, the system provides synchronized belief representations for action selection, effectively decoupling state estimation from the original policy optimization objective.

---

[Coalition Free Energy and Adaptive Precision in Multi-Agent Cooperation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26278)

- GT-FEP (Game-Theoretic Free Energy Principle): introduces a variational framework that models multi-agent coalition formation through a Gibbs distribution over interacting agents to address credit assignment under uncertainty.
- The framework utilizes APC (Adaptive Precision Control) to dynamically tune sensory precision, ensuring agents operate at an optimal inverted-U peak of credit assignment.
- By employing Harsanyi dividends for synergy-aware credit decomposition, the approach achieves robust coordination and emergent collective order in both real-world traffic datasets and multi-agent control tasks.

---

[SPEAR: Code-Augmented Agentic Prompt Optimization](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26275)

- SPEAR (Sandboxed Prompt Engineer with Active Rollback): introduces a free-form agentic optimizer that utilizes an Agent Brain, Python Sandbox, Evaluate Tool, Set Prompt Tool, Finish Tool, AgentState, Auto-rollback Guardrail, and Guard-metric Floor to autonomously improve prompts.
- The framework replaces fixed APE pipelines with an agent that actively authors analysis code over evaluation DataFrames to identify and resolve complex error patterns.
- SPEAR employs metric-based guardrails to ensure monotone improvement, effectively outperforming existing APE methods on industrial judge-prompt tasks by leveraging code-as-action capabilities.

---

[AgentSecBench: Measuring Prompt Injection, Privacy Leakage, and Tool-Use Integrity in LLM Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26269)

- AgentSecBench: introduces a formal security framework for LLM agents that measures instruction integrity, retrieval confidentiality, and capability integrity through intent-to-execution noninterference.
- The framework distinguishes between prompt annotation and enforcement by measuring pre-generation channel closure alongside adversarial advantage to evaluate defense effectiveness.
- AgentSecBench utilizes paired adversarial and benign-control executions to isolate security failures from model behavior, providing a rigorous method for auditing LLM agent security boundaries.

---

[Personalizing Embodied Multimodal Large Language Model Agents over Long-term User Interactions](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26256)

- POLAR: introduces a multimodal memory-augmented framework that organizes prior interactions into an object-centric knowledge graph to support personalized instance grounding and embodied planning.
- The framework utilizes a Memorization Module to convert raw interaction logs into structured semantic and episodic memories, which are then accessed by a Utilization Module to guide an MLLM agent's navigation decisions.
- By connecting personalized context across multiple episodes, POLAR enables agents to resolve implicit user intent and perform more accurate instance-level navigation compared to standard MLLM-based embodied agents.

---

[Sentinel: Embodied Cooperative Spatial Reasoning and Planning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26239)

- CoSaR: introduces a cooperative spatial reasoning and planning framework that integrates foundation models with classical navigation algorithms to enable decentralized embodied agents to coordinate in dynamic city-scale environments.
- The framework utilizes a structured spatial memory, including occupancy maps and ETA data, to support multi-agent communication, spatial reasoning, and collaborative trajectory replanning.
- CoSaR incorporates a multi-stage route refinement process and emergency avoidance mechanisms to ensure safe navigation while avoiding dynamic hazards modeled as sentinels.

---

[AgentSociety: Incentivizing Agentic Social Intelligence](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26203)

- AgentSociety: introduces a decentralized framework for multi-agent systems that leverages social choice theory to incentivize collaboration among self-interested agents through transitive vote delegation and strategic information diffusion.
- The framework models interactions as a Partially Observable Stochastic Game, where agents optimize individual utility by selectively disclosing competence signals to neighbors to influence routing paths.
- AgentSociety enables decentralized multi-task routing in heterogeneous LLM-based multi-agent systems by aligning individual strategic behavior with collective outcomes through fair, marginal-contribution-based payoffs.

---

[CyberEvolver: Structured Self-Evolution for Cybersecurity Agents On the Fly](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26195)

- CyberEvolver: introduces a self-evolving cybersecurity agent framework that iteratively revises its own scaffold using Strategy (LS), Environment Interface (LI), Domain Knowledge (LD), and Perception (LP) layers.
- The framework utilizes a Trajectory Diagnosis Pipeline to convert noisy execution logs into actionable revision signals for the Mutation Operator.
- CyberEvolver employs a population-based Beam Search strategy to maintain diverse agent variants and improve success rates across cybersecurity benchmarks.

---

[FuzzPilot: Plateau-Triggered Recipe Validation for Structured Text Fuzzing](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26539)

- FuzzPilot: introduces an off-hot-path controller that uses Ghidra headless, Model client, Plateau detector, Blackboard, Agents, and Micro-campaign runner to generate and validate mutation recipes for the AFL++ runner, Corpus, Custom mutator, and Target binary.
- The framework offloads expensive reasoning to an asynchronous controller, ensuring that the AFL++ hot path remains focused on high-throughput mutation using data-as-recipe objects.
- Preliminary evaluation on cJSON demonstrates that while the controller reduces plateau duration, the LLM-based proposal layer requires non-saturated targets to show measurable coverage improvements.

---

[ATOM: Instantiating Budget-Controllable Multi-Agent Collaboration via Nucleus-Electron Hierarchy](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26178)

- ATOM: introduces a hierarchical multi-agent framework that decouples a stable Nucleus (stable core reasoning backbone) from dynamic Electrons (dynamically activated peripheral agents) to optimize collaboration topologies based on task complexity.
- The framework utilizes a Complexity Predictor (estimates query difficulty for budgeting) to dynamically regulate agent instantiation, ensuring computational resources align with task demands.
- By employing a task-driven Reinforcement Learning Policy (optimizes task-driven collaboration graphs), ATOM achieves state-of-the-art performance while significantly improving token efficiency compared to static or fully generative multi-agent baselines.

---

[REPOMIRAGE: Probing Repository Context Reasoning in Code Agents with Perturbations](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26177)

- REPOMIRAGE: introduces a two-stage evaluation suite that uses semantics-preserving perturbations to probe repository context reasoning in LLMs, with REPOMIRAGE-Perturb, REPOMIRAGE-Extend, REPOANCHOR, and INSTRUCTIONS.md.
- The framework identifies a significant performance gap in LLMs when repository-level structural cues are obscured, revealing that agents often suffer from exploration drift.
- REPOANCHOR mitigates this by separating repository exploration from downstream problem solving, using an intermediate structural summary to anchor agent actions.

---

[A Universal Cliff and a Design Fingerprint: Cross-Section Defect Detection Under LLM Orchestration](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26174)

- LLM Orchestration Framework: introduces a study on how partitioning documents across multiple worker agents creates a universal "detection cliff" for cross-section defects that no single agent is positioned to identify.
- The framework utilizes a Controller to decompose tasks and an Integration Step to synthesize reports, revealing that even highly aligned LLMs exhibit an iatrogenic criterion shift where improved detection is coupled with increased false alarms.
- The research demonstrates that at the "bottom of the cliff," LLMs may exhibit anosodiaphoria, where they accurately perceive structural defects in private internal-state probes but fail to weight them as reportable issues in the final integrated output.

---

[ESBMC: A Survey of Its Evolution, Integration, and Future Directions in Formal Software Verification](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26169)

- ESBMC: introduces a comprehensive survey of the Efficient SMT-based Context-Bounded Model Checker, detailing its evolution from a research prototype to an industrially capable verification platform integrating Language Front-ends, GOTO program IR, BMC-k-induction encoder, SSA layer, SMT solver portfolio, Verification Engine, and Witness Generator.
- The framework leverages a modular architecture where Language Front-ends translate diverse source code into a common GOTO program IR, which is then processed by the BMC-k-induction encoder and SSA layer before being dispatched to an SMT solver portfolio for formal verification.
- Recent advancements include the integration of LLMs for automated vulnerability repair, loop invariant generation, and agentic model checking, establishing ESBMC as a natively autonomous verification kernel for safety-critical systems.

---

#### 24th May 2026

[Eureka: Intelligent Feature Engineering for Enterprise AI Cloud Resource Demand Prediction](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25297)

- Eureka: introduces an agentic framework for automated feature engineering that treats feature creation as an iterative code generation problem to improve predictive performance.
- The framework utilizes an Eureka Expert for planning, an LLM Feature Factory for code generation, and a Self-Evolving Alignment Engine for reinforcement learning-based refinement.
- Eureka integrates domain knowledge and feedback loops to generate executable, domain-aligned features, demonstrating significant improvements in cloud GPU resource demand forecasting.

---

[PR-Aware Automated Unit Test Generation: Challenges and Opportunities](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25285)

- PR-Aware Automated Unit Test Generation Framework: evaluates the effectiveness of EvoSuite and GPT-4o in generating fail-to-pass (F2P) unit tests for pull requests by analyzing their ability to capture specific code changes.
- The study utilizes a curated dataset of 353 Java 8 pull requests to compare traditional search-based test generation against single-prompt LLM-based approaches.
- Results indicate that while EvoSuite is more reliable, both tools struggle to generate meaningful tests for pull requests, highlighting the need for feedback-driven, agentic workflows in automated testing.

---

[LLM-as-a-Judge in Healthcare: A Scoping Analysis of Applications, Methods, and Human Alignment](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25273)

- LLM-as-a-Judge Framework: introduces a systematic review of LLM-based evaluation methods in healthcare, characterizing how LLMs are configured as judges using Prompt Engineering, Ensemble, RAG, Calibration, Fine-tuning, Multi-agent, and Distillation.
- The paper categorizes healthcare applications into clinical decision support, clinical NLP, medical knowledge and QA, and medical communication, while evaluating judge performance through rubrics and human-alignment metrics.
- The study identifies key failure modes such as evaluation bias from shared model families, conflation of surface presentation with substantive quality, and hallucination, emphasizing the need for rigorous validation and human oversight in clinical deployment.

---

[LipoAgent: Coordinating Fine-Tuned LLM Agents for Safer Lipid Design](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25250)

- LipoAgent: introduces a safety-aware multi-agent LLM framework that integrates domain-specific fine-tuning, conditional prediction, and multi-agent verification to optimize lipid discovery for mRNA delivery.
- The framework utilizes a Predictor Agent and a Verifier Agent to jointly model toxicity and transfection efficiency, ensuring that toxicity is treated as a decision-level constraint rather than a post hoc filter.
- By incorporating a human-in-the-loop mechanism for ambiguous cases, LipoAgent achieves high predictive accuracy and reliability while significantly reducing the time and effort required for experimental lipid screening.

---

[FrontierOR: Benchmarking LLMs’ Capacity for Efficient Algorithm Design in Large-Scale Optimization](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25246)

- FrontierOR: introduces a benchmark for evaluating LLMs on large-scale optimization algorithm design, utilizing LLM Generation, Evaluation Protocol, Tiny Instances, Feasibility Checker, Baseline Compare, Expert Audit, Agent Repair, Self-evolving Frameworks, and Shared Memory to assess algorithmic efficiency beyond simple formulation.
- The framework evaluates LLMs by requiring them to infer problem structure from natural-language descriptions and generate executable algorithms that compete with expert-verified Gurobi baselines.
- FrontierOR includes 180 literature-grounded tasks and employs self-evolving agents to iteratively refine candidate programs, demonstrating that test-time search significantly improves solution quality and computational efficiency.

---

[Meta-Agent: From Task Descriptions to Verified Multi-Agent Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25233)

- Meta-Agent: introduces a two-phase framework that automatically constructs and executes specialized multi-agent systems from natural-language task descriptions.
- The framework utilizes a construction phase to generate a directed acyclic graph of agent specifications and an execution phase that employs continuous verification to prevent cascading errors.
- It incorporates a three-level error attribution mechanism to distinguish between local, upstream, and structural failures, enabling targeted recovery strategies for improved workflow stability.

---

[K8S Power Irrigation: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Performance-Aware Power Efficiency of Kubernetes Cloud-Native Microservices](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25218)

- K8SPI (Kubernetes Power Irrigation): introduces a hierarchical reinforcement learning controller that jointly optimizes CPU core and uncore frequencies to minimize power consumption in cloud-native environments while maintaining strict performance requirements.
- The framework utilizes a two-stage control architecture consisting of a Coarse-grained Agent for rapid performance violation mitigation and a Fine-grained Agent for iterative power minimization.
- K8SPI incorporates a multi-level telemetry stack and an offline Config-Action-Observation Snapshot Cache to enable efficient, hardware-in-the-loop reinforcement learning training without live-hardware latency.

---

[GroupTravelBench: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Multi-Person Travel Planning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25200)

- GroupTravelBench: introduces a comprehensive benchmark for multi-user, multi-turn travel planning that evaluates an agent's ability to perform preference elicitation, conflict coordination, and collective planning using Task synthesis pipeline, Multi-party interaction framework, Evaluation protocol, Tool sandbox, User simulators, and LLM judge.
- The framework utilizes a synchronous group-chat environment where one agent interacts with multiple LLM-based user simulators to resolve conflicting preferences and generate feasible itineraries.
- Evaluation is conducted through a combination of rule-based outcome metrics and an LLM-based process assessment to provide a fine-grained analysis of agent performance in real-world group travel scenarios.

---

[Hide to Guide: Learning via Semantic Masking](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25198)

- SMEPO (Semantic Masked Expert Policy Optimization): introduces a fine-grained semantic masking strategy for expert-guided RLVR that converts expert traces into fill-in-the-blank guidance by masking reward-relevant spans while preserving procedural scaffolding.
- The framework prevents reward hacking in LLMs by hiding verifier-aligned content—such as final answers, executable code, or critical entities—along the critical path of expert traces.
- SMEPO improves accuracy and convergence efficiency across math, code, and agentic search domains by forcing the policy to reconstruct missing information rather than simply copying exposed expert cues.

---

[DarkForest: Less Talk, Higher Accuracy for Multi-Agent LLMs](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25188)

- DarkForest: introduces a controlled-communication coordination framework that aggregates outputs from multiple LLMs by generating candidates independently, constructing a calibrated belief state, and disclosing only compact, policy-permitted evidence to a coordinator.
- The framework utilizes a belief construction mechanism that weights agent support based on historical reliability, parse quality, confidence, and independence corrections to mitigate error propagation and reduce communication overhead.
- A deterministic guardrail provides a final safety check by comparing the coordinator's output against the calibrated belief state, ensuring that strongly supported candidates are not discarded due to single-model errors.

---

[Grow-Prune-Freeze Networks: Adaptive &amp; Continual Learning Technique for Olfactory Navigation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25170)

- GPF (Grow-Prune-Freeze) Networks: introduces an adaptive continual learning framework that dynamically grows, prunes, and freezes neural network layers to maintain stability and plasticity in non-stationary environments.
- The framework utilizes Expected SARSA for policy optimization and grounds its structural adaptation in random matrix theory to ensure spectral stability during layer growth.
- GPF networks demonstrate effective performance in partially observable olfactory navigation tasks and show potential for generalization across reinforcement learning, image classification, and autoregressive LLM tasks.

---

[STREAM: A Data-Centric Framework for Mining High-Value Task-Oriented Dialogues from Streaming Media](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25162)

- Stream: introduces a data-centric framework that mines high-value interaction signals from streaming media to synthesize complex task-oriented dialogues using SSI, APS, CB, and IDG.
- The framework leverages RAG-enhanced UUS and ARG to generate realistic, goal-oriented conversations grounded in UPM and APM, while employing DF to ensure dataset quality.
- StreamDial, the resulting large-scale dataset, improves intrinsic dialogue quality and downstream DST performance across multiple domains and languages under controlled training budgets.

---

[SimuWoB: Simulating Real-World Mobile Apps for Fast and Faithful GUI Agent Benchmarking](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25160)

- SimuWoB: introduces a fully synthetic benchmark for mobile GUI agents that leverages a Code-generation LLM, PRD Generator, Page Structure Generator, Data Entity Generator, Task-injection Agent, Agent Validator, Manual Check, and Bundle Pool to create high-fidelity, backend-free mobile app environments for efficient evaluation.
- The framework utilizes a two-stage synthesis pipeline where the first stage constructs a minimal working environment and the second stage injects tasks and validators, ensuring both functional completeness and precise reward calculation.
- Comprehensive experiments on state-of-the-art mobile GUI agents reveal significant performance gaps in long-horizon tasks, highlighting the necessity for improved memory, proactive exploration, and fine-grained control in future agent development.

---

[LLM-Agent-Based Renewable Energy Forecasting Using Edge and IoT Data: A Review of Solar, Wind, Weather, and Grid-Aware Decision Support](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25141)

- LLM-Agent-Based Renewable Energy Forecasting Framework: introduces a six-layer taxonomy for integrating IoT edge data with LLM agents to enhance renewable energy forecasting through IoT Edge Devices, Data Acquisition Layer, Preprocessing Layer, Feature Engineering Layer, Model Inference Layer, Uncertainty Estimation Layer, and LLM Agent Reasoning and Reporting Layer.
- The framework leverages LLMs to synthesize heterogeneous sensor streams and grid constraints into actionable, human-readable decision support for grid operators.
- This review identifies critical research gaps including decision-aligned forecasting, real-time model adaptation, and the mitigation of LLM hallucinations in safety-critical grid environments.

---

[Trust but Verify: Prover-Verifier Deliberation for Selective LLM Prediction](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25133)

- PVD (Prover-Verifier Deliberation): introduces an inference-time protocol for selective prediction where a Prover defends a candidate answer through atomic sub-claims while a Verifier issues targeted challenges to produce an Accept + No Change (ANC) confidence signal.
- The framework utilizes a Prover (LLM generating structured proofs) and a Verifier (LLM issuing targeted challenges) to empirically evaluate argument defensibility rather than relying on simple sample agreement.
- PVD (Prover-Verifier Deliberation): provides a robust diagnostic for LLM reliability by identifying when a Verifier lacks the domain knowledge to sustain adversarial pressure, indicated by the collapse of the ANC accuracy gap.

---

[Multi-Agent Specification-based Metamorphic Testing of FMU-Based Simulations](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25101)

- AgenticMeta: introduces an LLM-powered multi-agent workflow for the automated metamorphic testing of FMU-based simulation models, utilizing Coordinator Node, Extractor Agent, MR Generator Agent, MR Refiner Agent, Test Generator Agent, Test Validator Agent, Test Executor, Centralised Storage, Model Description Parser, and Functional Description Markdown Generator.
- The framework automates the entire testing pipeline by extracting requirements from functional specifications, generating and refining metamorphic relations, and executing metamorphic tests on simulation models.
- The approach employs a hub-and-spoke architecture where specialized LLM-based agents perform distinct tasks, while deterministic tools handle simulation execution and mutation-based quality assessment.

---

[AgentIR: A Workload-Adaptive Cascade Retrieval Substrate for Long-Term Conversational Memory](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25092)

- AgentIR: introduces a workload-adaptive retrieval substrate for LLM agents that optimizes memory access by dynamically selecting between sparse, dense, and temporal retrieval channels based on query type and confidence.
- The framework utilizes a time-partitioned index to achieve sub-linear scaling under extreme recency bias, ensuring sub-millisecond latency for growing conversational memory.
- AgentIR employs a cascade router to skip expensive dense retrieval channels for high-confidence sparse queries, significantly reducing amortized latency while maintaining LLM-judged retrieval accuracy.

---

[Evolutionary Enhanced Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Cooperative Air Combat](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25091)

- ACE-MAPPO: introduces a hybrid learning framework that integrates evolutionary algorithms with MAPPO to enhance exploration, sample efficiency, and policy generalization in multi-aircraft cooperative air combat.
- The framework utilizes a genetic soft update mechanism for population-based exploration, an evolutionary-augmented prioritized trajectory replay for efficient sample utilization, and an adversarial curriculum module for adaptive difficulty scheduling.
- Experimental results demonstrate that ACE-MAPPO outperforms baseline MARL methods in training stability, convergence speed, and win rate within complex, non-stationary BVR air combat environments.

---

[Security in the Fine-Tuning Lifecycle of Large Language Models: Threats, Defenses, Evaluation, and Future Directions](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25073)

- Fine-tuning Security Framework: introduces a systematic lifecycle-based approach to categorize and evaluate security threats and defenses across Pre-tuning Phase, During-tuning Phase, and Post-tuning Phase.
- The framework maps attack surfaces including Training Data, Model Parameters, Adapters, and FTaaS Interface to corresponding defensive strategies for maintaining safety alignment and mitigating backdoors.
- This survey provides a unified empirical evaluation of representative attack and defense methods, revealing that security effectiveness is highly dependent on model architecture, alignment status, and the specific fine-tuning lifecycle stage.

---

[Cultivating Machine Intelligence: The OMEGA Shift from Top-Down Optimization to Autopoietic Cognitive Ecologies](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25062)

- RECLAIM (Recursive, Ecological, Cognitive, Lifelike, Adaptive, Intelligent Machine): introduces a theoretical framework for cultivating machine intelligence through computational ecology, utilizing Autopoietic Unit, Sensory Boundary, Internal Network, Active Boundary, Energy Budget, Toroidal Lattice, Data Streams, and Cognitive Food Chain.
- The framework replaces top-down gradient-based optimization with bottom-up evolutionary dynamics, where intelligence emerges from the thermodynamic interaction of autopoietic units within a resource-constrained environment.
- RECLAIM addresses structural failure modes in LLMs by eliminating proxy objectives, instead relying on environmental physics and Polya-Hebbian dynamics to drive path-dependent specialization and open-ended complexity.

---

[Aion∞: Next-Generation Tasks and Practical Harness for Time Series](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25045)

- Aion∞: introduces a specialized harness for time series tasks that integrates agents, skills, rules, memory, evaluation, and protocols to ensure reliable, constraint-aware problem solving.
- The framework organizes execution around three design principles: temporal grounding, temporal knowledge-grounded reasoning, and reliability mechanisms to maintain temporal constraints and evidence integrity.
- Aion∞ includes planning-, orchestration-, interpretation-, evidence collection-, construction-, and review-agents that govern the execution loop to prevent premature convergence and ensure rigorous validation.

---

[MimirRAG: A Multi-Agent RAG Framework for Financial Data Retrieval with Metadata Integration](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25030)

- MimirRAG: introduces a modular, multi-agent RAG framework designed for financial data retrieval, utilizing Extractor Agent, Planner Agent, Search Agent, Validator Agent, Writer Agent, and Conversation Agent to improve retrieval precision and reasoning.
- The framework integrates structured metadata and table-aware chunking via Docling and Chonkie to enhance context quality for LLMs in financial analysis.
- MimirRAG employs an iterative Action Design Research (ADR) methodology, incorporating expert feedback from financial analysts to calibrate trust and improve system usability.

---

[Micro-Swarm Locomotion Optimization in Dynamic Flow using Multi-Objective Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25025)

- CFD-MO-MARL: introduces a hybrid framework coupling high-fidelity Navier-Stokes solvers with multi-objective multi-agent reinforcement learning to optimize micro-swarm locomotion in oscillatory fluid environments.
- The framework utilizes a Centralized Training, Decentralized Execution paradigm where agents learn policies reconciled by PCGrad to balance competing objectives of upstream progress, energy efficiency, and motion smoothness.
- The research demonstrates that gradient surgery is a structural requirement for stable convergence in this domain, enabling the emergence of complex collective behaviors like flow-cycle-aware ratcheting and hydrodynamic throttling.

---

[A perspective on fluid mechanical environments for challenges in reinforcement learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25011)

- Fluid mechanical environments for reinforcement learning: introduces fluid mechanics as a robust testbed for training RL agents to interact with high-dimensional, nonstationary environments by leveraging preserved physical invariances.
- The paper demonstrates how agents can utilize the Navier-Stokes equations as a stable world model while navigating complex, evolving flow dynamics generated by simulators like Dedalus.
- It highlights the potential of surrogate models to accelerate environment simulation, facilitating the development of RL agents capable of operating in scientific and industrial flow contexts.

---

[ClueAegis: Heuristic-to-Reasoning Cognitive-skill Learning for Unified Evidence-based Synthetic Image Detection](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25009)

- ClueAegis: introduces a two-stage agentic framework that reformulates synthetic image detection as a heuristic-to-reasoning process by decomposing forensic analysis into specialized cognitive skills.
- The framework utilizes a perception-driven agent to select the most relevant forensic skill from a predefined library, followed by skill-conditioned reasoning to extract evidence and perform authenticity classification.
- ClueAegis incorporates a large-scale benchmark, ClueAegis-Bench, and employs supervised fine-tuning and GRPO-based cognitive alignment to enhance reasoning consistency and cross-domain generalization.

---

[Meta-Modal Agent: Sequential Evidence Routing for Missing-Modality Candidate Reranking](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25007)

- MMA (Meta-Modal Agent): introduces a candidate-pool reranking framework that treats missing modalities as a sequential evidence-routing problem, utilizing an LLM-based agent to dynamically query available tools and fuse scores with first-stage retrieval.
- The framework employs balanced missingness-task training with PPO to ensure the agent learns to effectively route and recover from failed tool calls across various missing-modality patterns.
- MMA-Auto improves target-positive OOMA NDCG@10 by 4.0% over strong static baselines, while MMA-Interactive provides an upper-bound diagnostic through user clarification.

---

[MEMMARK: State-Evolution Attribution Watermarking for Agent Long-Term Memory Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.25002)

- MEMMARK: introduces a backend-invariant watermarking framework that embeds provenance into latent memory-evolution decisions to ensure robust attribution for long-term agent memory.
- The framework utilizes a Backend Adapter, Boundary Interception, Keyed Sampler, Memory Update, and Cryptographic Audit Trace to enable verifiable provenance even when external logs are unavailable.
- MEMMARK preserves memory utility and provides snapshot-only attribution by binding memory-write decisions to reproducible, keyed behavioral traces.

---

[Scaling up Energy-Aware Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Mission-Oriented Drone Networks with Individual Reward](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24992)

- Energy-Aware MARL: introduces a collaborative execution framework for mission-oriented drone networks that utilizes DQN with individual reward functions to optimize trajectory planning and task execution under battery constraints.
- The framework incorporates an energy model to account for drone battery capacity, ensuring that agents learn efficient policies to complete tasks and return to the base station safely.
- By replacing shared rewards with individual reward functions, the model mitigates credit assignment issues, resulting in improved robustness and scalability in dynamic environments compared to traditional shared reward approaches.

---

[Integrated Sensing, Communication, and Computing for NR-V2X: A Cross-Layer Resource Allocation Framework Using Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24972)

- MAPPO-SPS (Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization-based Sensing-based Semi-Persistent Scheduling): introduces a cross-layer framework for NR-V2X Mode 2 that jointly optimizes sensing, communication, and computation resources using Vehicular Agents, MEC-enabled RSU, Cloud, PC5 Sidelink, V2I Uplink, Local CPU, MEC Server, Centralized Critic, and Decentralized Actors.
- The framework models the scheduling problem as a cooperative partially observable Markov game, utilizing a centralized-training decentralized-execution (CTDE) paradigm to balance sensing accuracy, communication reliability, and computation latency.
- Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves superior performance in packet reception ratio, sensing accuracy, and MEC queueing delay compared to greedy and non-learning baselines under dense vehicular traffic.

---

[Towards Multi-Turn Dialog Systems for Industrial Asset Operations and Maintenance](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24953)

- Supervisor-Specialist Multi-Agent System: introduces a multi-turn dialog architecture for industrial operations and maintenance that utilizes a supervisor agent to coordinate multiple specialist agents and reuse structured artifacts.
- The framework incorporates three distinct layers, including a user interaction layer, an agent orchestration layer, and an MCP tools and data resources layer, to facilitate efficient diagnostic workflows.
- By implementing cross-turn artifact reuse and dynamic replanning, the system significantly reduces redundant tool invocations and improves task completion rates compared to traditional single-agent plan-execute architectures.

---

[APT-Agent: Automated Penetration Testing using Large Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24949)

- APT-Agent: introduces a fully automated penetration testing framework that leverages LLMs to orchestrate reconnaissance, exploitation, and exfiltration while mitigating hallucinations and memory loss.
- The framework integrates a hybrid rectification module for command validation and a stage-aware context management module to preserve operational history across multi-step attack sequences.
- Evaluations on Metasploitable 2 demonstrate that APT-Agent achieves an 84.29% end-to-end exploitation success rate, significantly outperforming prior LLM-based penetration testing approaches.

---

[Memory-Induced Tool-Drift in LLM Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24941)

- MEMDRIFT: introduces a systematic failure mode where personality-driven biases stored in long-term memory inappropriately influence tool-call parameter selection in professional contexts.
- The study demonstrates that biased memories act as implicit steering vectors, hijacking attention through lexical overlap and pushing activations along latent directions that mirror explicit behavioral instructions.
- Evaluations across seven frontier LLMs and multiple production memory architectures confirm that this vulnerability is pervasive and persists despite standard prompt-based or filtering-based defenses.

---

[ProActor: Timing-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Proactive Task Scheduling Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24900)

- ProActor: introduces a unified framework for conversational task scheduling that optimizes proactive timing and action consistency using turn-level reinforcement learning.
- The framework integrates an automated annotation pipeline, a suite of proactiveness metrics, and the ART-F training infrastructure to enable efficient RL in GPU-constrained environments.
- ProActor utilizes composite rewards and GRPO to balance proactive timing with action consistency, outperforming static prompting-based baselines.

---

[Unifying Value Alignment and Assignment in Cross-Domain Offline Reinforcement Learning with Heterogeneous Datasets](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24862)

- V2A: introduces a framework for heterogeneous cross-domain offline RL that integrates dynamics alignment, value alignment, and value assignment to mitigate value misassignment.
- The framework employs temporally-consistent modality representation learning to infer underlying dynamics and modality-aware advantage learning to rectify sample optimality assessment.
- V2A utilizes a data filtering paradigm to selectively share source samples, significantly improving target policy performance in heterogeneous settings compared to existing baselines.

---

[Macaron-A2UI: A Model for Generative UI in Personal Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24830)

- Macaron-A2UI: introduces a unified framework for generating lightweight, executable UI actions alongside natural language responses in personal agents using a declarative protocol.
- The approach utilizes a two-stage training pipeline, combining LoRA-based supervised fine-tuning with group-relative policy optimization to internalize UI generation capabilities without requiring long schema prompts.
- The framework includes a comprehensive evaluation benchmark, A2UI-Bench, which assesses protocol validity, task construction quality, and user experience through both LLM-based and VLM-based judges.

---

[Test-Time Deep Thinking to Explore Implicit Rules](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24828)

- TTExplore (Test-Time Exploration): introduces a framework that decouples low-level action execution from high-level strategic reasoning to enable LLMs to infer implicit environmental rules during test-time interaction.
- The framework utilizes an Actor (primary agent generating actions) and a Thinker (meta-level reasoning agent) to analyze the History Trajectory (sequence of observations and actions) and generate Deep Thoughts (reasoning about implicit rules) to guide exploration.
- To optimize the Thinker, the authors implement a stable reinforcement learning pipeline that uses indirect task-level rewards and retains only a single thinking node per trajectory to mitigate reward sparsity and training instability.

---

[Agent Manufacturing: Foundation-Model Agents as First-Class Industrial Entities](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24823)

- Agent Manufacturing: introduces a paradigm shift where foundation-model agents perform coordinative cognition in industrial systems, utilizing Design Agent, Process Agent, Scheduling Agent, Machine Agents, Quality Agent, Maintenance Agent, Memory System, and Human Governance Interface.
- The framework replaces human-centric coordinative cognition with autonomous reasoning agents capable of open-vocabulary goal interpretation, long-horizon planning, and inter-agent negotiation.
- This paradigm distinguishes itself from previous industrial revolutions by targeting the high-rent coordinative layer of production rather than physical or routine-cognitive tasks.

---

[CoRe-Code: Collaborative Reinforcement Learning for Code Generation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24812)

- CoRe-Code: introduces a role-specialized multi-agent framework that utilizes Collaborative GRPO to align a Planner Agent (generates high-level algorithmic plans) and a Coder Agent (translates plans into executable code) through Verifiable Execution Feedback (downstream code execution results).
- The framework employs Algorithmic Thought (structured plan representation) to provide actionable guidance, while a Memory Efficiency Reward (optimizes for minimal memory usage) ensures the generated code is both accurate and computationally efficient.
- CoRe-Code demonstrates flexibility by extending its reinforcement learning approach to optimize other agents, such as Retrieval and Debugging agents, within broader multi-agent systems.

---

[Fishbone: From One 3D Asset to a Million Controllable Edits](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24805)

- Fishbone: introduces a unified framework that constructs a coupled rib-spine representation from an input mesh to enable diverse, controllable 3D shape variations through intuitive low-dimensional parameters.
- The framework automatically extracts Ribs, Spine, and Skinning Weights from general meshes, supporting rib-driven and spine-driven deformation primitives, reduced-space dynamics, and keyframe animation.
- Fishbone further enables downstream applications including image-conditioned 3D generation, robot-learning data augmentation, and agentic mesh editing via a VLM agent.

---

[PANDO: Efficient Multimodal AI Agents via Online Skill Distillation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24785)

- PANDO: introduces a single-rollout online skill-distillation framework that improves agent efficiency by building a structured Skill Library through Planner, Skill Selector, Actor, Reflector, and Learning Module components.
- The framework utilizes Visual Compression and Cache-aware Prompting to reduce marginal token load as the agent accumulates experience across tasks.
- PANDO achieves state-of-the-art success rates on VisualWebArena while significantly reducing token consumption compared to existing baseline methods.

---

[From Replacement to Orchestration: A Socio-Technical Architecture for Agentic AI in Corporate R&amp;D](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24580)

- HARMONY (Hybrid Agentic Research Model for Organisational New Yield): introduces a four-pillar socio-technical architecture designed to transition corporate R&amp;D from a replacement-focused model to an orchestration-based system using ResOps, Control Tower, Ethics Fabric, and Talent Studio.
- The framework addresses the R&amp;D productivity paradox by redistributing cognitive load from human researchers to autonomous agents while maintaining strategic alignment through the Sciencepreneur archetype.
- HARMONY mitigates Research Debt by implementing structural governance via the Ethics Fabric and real-time telemetry through the Control Tower to ensure agentic systems remain aligned with organizational goals.

---

[Code2UML: Agentic LLMs with context engineering for scalable software visualization](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24453)

- Code2UML: introduces an agentic architecture that utilizes context engineering and a hierarchy of specialized agents to generate scalable, syntactically valid UML diagrams directly from source code repositories.
- The framework employs a deterministic, importance-weighted IR compaction layer to transform large codebases into compact, diagram-specific views that fit within LLM context constraints without requiring additional LLM calls.
- The system features a multi-agent hierarchy including planner-, analyzer-, generator-, corrector- and dependency-agents, which collaborate to produce high-quality UML diagrams across multiple programming languages and diagram types.

---

[A Unified Python Framework for Direct PPO-based Control of AHUs with Economizer Logic and CO2-Constrained Ventilation](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24406)

- PPO-based HVAC Control Framework: introduces a unified Python-based environment that integrates 2R-2C thermal physics and dynamic CO2 mass balance to optimize building energy efficiency and indoor air quality.
- The framework utilizes a PPO agent to modulate fan speeds while employing Hierarchical Flow Logic to ensure CO2 levels remain below safety thresholds and an enthalpy-based economizer for free cooling.
- Experimental results demonstrate that the PPO-based approach achieves superior temperature stability and approximately 6% energy savings compared to traditional PID and on/off control strategies.

---

[Tool-Schema Compression Enables Agentic RAG Under Constrained Context Budgets](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26165)

- TSCG (Deterministic tool-schema compilation): introduces a systematic study of the tool-context trade-off in agentic RAG, demonstrating that structure-aware schema compression is a necessary infrastructure layer for constrained-context deployments.
- The framework utilizes a ReAct-style agent loop and a context budget model to evaluate how compressed tool definitions enable functional RAG performance by freeing context for retrieval chunks.
- Research findings establish that TSCG compression restores RAG functionality at tight context budgets and identifies a distractor dilution effect where excessive retrieval chunks can degrade performance in smaller LLMs.

---

[When Does Adaptive Guidance Help? Belief-Aware Privileged Distillation for Autonomous Driving Under Partial Observability](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26155)

- BA-GSAC (Belief-Aware Guided Soft Actor-Critic): introduces a teacher-student framework that dynamically modulates distillation coefficients using epistemic uncertainty derived from an ensemble of forward dynamics models.
- The framework utilizes a Guiding actor, Control actor, Twin Q-network, Ensemble of forward dynamics models, Distillation DNN, Replay buffer, and Warmup buffer to improve autonomous driving performance under partial observability.
- The research identifies an observability blindness phenomenon where ensemble-based uncertainty estimation fails under severe occlusion due to the use of partial observation prediction targets.

---

[MemMorph: Tool Hijacking in LLM Agents via Memory Poisoning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26154)

- MemMorph: introduces a memory poisoning attack that compromises LLM agent tool selection by injecting crafted records into long-term memory to steer reasoning toward attacker-preferred tools.
- The framework utilizes Potential Query Modeling, Structured Memory Initialization, and Constrained Memory Optimization to ensure poisoned records are both retrievable and effective while remaining indistinguishable from legitimate experience.
- MemMorph demonstrates high attack success rates across multiple LLM backbones and memory modules, proving that long-term memory is a critical, under-explored attack surface for tool-augmented agents.

---

#### 23rd May 2026

[GRAIL: AI translation for scientists application workflow on satellite data](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24784)

- GRAIL: introduces an agentic translation system that converts Python geospatial workflows into scalable Spark-based programs by adapting the RDPro library for LLM-readiness.
- The framework utilizes a LangGraph pipeline to decompose code generation into structured sections, incorporating Task Analyzer, Planner, Code Generator, and Validation and Repair Agent components.
- To improve LLM performance, the system implements LLM-ready RDPro Documentation, API Alias Functions, and Detailed Error Logs to provide precise context and actionable feedback during the iterative repair loop.

---

[PRIMA: Operational Patterns for Resilient Multi-Agent Research with Verifiable Identity and Convergent Feedback](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24775)

- PRIMA: introduces a multi-agent research system designed to survive long-running failure modes through resilience-and-recovery layer, sub-agent operating discipline, and structured engineering pipeline.
- The framework utilizes a prime-power identity scheme for verifiable agent membership and a dual-metric scoring engine to ensure convergence through both LLM-judged rubrics and sandboxed code execution.
- PRIMA incorporates a meta-optimization loop to evolve research programs and an event-driven persistence layer to support complex branching and auditability in multi-hour autonomous research tasks.

---

[Spectral Retrieval: Multi-Scale Sinc Convolution over Token Embeddings for Localized Retrieval in LLM Multi-Agent Systems](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24764)

- Spectral Retrieval: introduces a plug-in re-ranking method that improves retrieval precision by applying multi-scale sinc convolutions to per-token embeddings, effectively bridging the gap between mean-pooled and per-token MaxSim approaches.
- The framework utilizes a multi-scale aggregator to identify localized relevance within documents, significantly outperforming standard mean-pooling on benchmarks requiring precise subspan matching.
- Designed for LLM multi-agent systems, the approach provides specialized retrieval windows for individual agents, facilitating more effective consensus-based reasoning by a debate orchestrator.

---

[Drift-Resistant Navigation World Model with Anchored Epipolar Guidance](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24761)

- DR-NWM: introduces a generative world model that mitigates perceptual and geometric drift in navigation by utilizing an anchor-guided rollout strategy and bidirectional epipolar constraints.
- The framework employs an AC-DiT (Anchor-conditioned Diffusion Transformer) to generate intermediate frames by conditioning on past context and future anchors, effectively partitioning long-horizon rollouts into shorter, stable chunks.
- By integrating bidirectional epipolar masks into the cross-attention layers, the model enforces geometric consistency and spatial grounding without requiring explicit 3D supervision.

---

[Proper Scoring Rules for Agentic Uncertainty Quantification](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24756)

- TPS: introduces a predictor-agnostic family of strictly proper trajectory-level scoring rules for evaluating LLM agent uncertainty signals throughout a trajectory.
- The framework extends evaluation to administratively censored trajectories by projecting complete-data scores onto observable prefixes using a continuation-success weight.
- Empirical results demonstrate that TPS effectively detects probability-scale errors in LLM agents that rank-based metrics like AUROC fail to identify.

---

[Automated Detection and Classification of Delusion-related Content in Naturalistic Audio Diaries Using Multi-Agent Language Models](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24755)

- Multi-Agent Clinical Delusion Annotation Pipeline: introduces an automated framework for multi-label extraction of delusional themes, affective responses, and behavioral responses from naturalistic audio diary transcripts using EMA Data, Progressive Prompting, LLM Inference, and Adjudication Strategies.
- The pipeline utilizes an ensemble of three LLMs (GLM-4.6, GPT-OSS, and Qwen-3) to perform independent annotations, which are then resolved through various Adjudication Strategies to improve classification accuracy.
- Empirical results demonstrate that while Progressive Prompting reduces false positives, complex conversational debate can degrade performance on ambiguous clinical text, whereas majority voting provides the most robust consensus.

---

[Facility Location Mechanism Design - Breaking The Deterministic Barrier](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24750)

- RR-CWM (Randomly Rotated Coordinate-wise Median): introduces a strategyproof mechanism that breaks the deterministic approximation barrier for facility location by applying a random rotation before computing the coordinate-wise median.
- The framework utilizes a Random Rotation Matrix to eliminate axis-dependence, a Coordinate-wise Median to minimize social cost in rotated space, and an Inverse Rotation to return the facility to the original coordinate system.
- The paper further extends this approach to the learning-augmented setting via RR-CMP, which incorporates machine-learned predictions to achieve an improved consistency-robustness trade-off compared to deterministic baselines.

---

["It Felt a Bit Eerie": Exploring Humanlike Interactions During Collaborative Writing with an Artificial Agent](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24729)

- AI-assisted text editor framework: introduces a collaborative writing system that manipulates temporal and visual humanlike cues to examine their impact on user experience and social perception.
- The framework utilizes an LLM-backend to provide autonomous or user-prompted suggestions, which are presented via an Editing Pane and optionally visualized through an agentic cursor.
- The study evaluates three variants of the framework, finding that while humanlike design can increase perceived collaboration, it also introduces social costs such as feelings of surveillance and mental overload.

---

[Hylos: Operability Contracts for Model-Native Spatial Intelligence](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24728)

- Hylos: introduces a systems architecture for contract-bounded spatial intelligence that treats spatial state as a transaction-safe operable substrate using a Scene Operability Graph, Evidence Set, Admissible Actuator Set, Capability Gaps, Protected Invariants, Realization Projections, Runtime Knowledge, SpatialTransaction, Latent Execution Auditor, and Parametric Neural Edit Handles.
- The framework ensures that generated or edited 3D artifacts are inspectable, modifiable, validated, and provenance-backed by requiring all changes to pass through a formal SpatialTransaction boundary.
- Hylos enables reliable agentic interaction by routing visible symptoms through semantic dependency structures to validated upstream causes rather than applying direct, unverified geometric edits.

---

[Streaming Reinforcement Learning under Partial Observability with Real-Time Recurrent Learning](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24709)

- RTU-RTRL: introduces a streaming reinforcement learning method for partially observable environments by integrating Recurrent Trace Units with exact Real-Time Recurrent Learning to enable efficient credit assignment without replay buffers.
- The framework utilizes a diagonal recurrent architecture to achieve linear time and memory complexity, allowing the RTRL sensitivity matrix to propagate credit through time and recurrent states simultaneously.
- A first-order Taylor correction is implemented to mitigate staleness in the sensitivity matrix, ensuring stable learning performance under sustained credit-assignment requirements.

---

[TS-Skill: A Benchmark for Evaluating Analytical Skills in Time-Series Question Answering](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24703)

- SKEvol (Skill-guided agentic framework): introduces a skill-guided agentic framework that constructs the TS-Skill benchmark by generating timestamped time-series seeds and evolving them into verified QA pairs through multi-phase verification.
- The framework utilizes a proposal agent to generate questions and answers, which are then validated by a multi-component verifier agent including VLM-, rule-based-, and human-in-the-loop-review modules.
- SKEvol supports both metadata-assisted and code-assisted answer construction, enabling the evaluation of complex temporal reasoning skills such as scale selection, localization, and cross-interval integration.

---

[MDIA: A Multi-Agent Diagnostic Intelligence Pipeline on HealthBench Professional](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24699)

- MDIA (Multi-agent Diagnostic Intelligence Agent): introduces a 7-node specialty-routed clinical reasoning graph architecture that leverages multi-turn context preservation and engine-level reliability to outperform existing LLM-based clinical systems on the HealthBench Professional benchmark.
- The framework utilizes an Intake orchestrator, a specialty Router, domain-specific reasoners (Gi_reasoner, Ophtho_reasoner, Neuro_reasoner), a generalist Reasoning agent, an Output synthesizer, and a Verifier to ensure clinical safety and formatting.
- MDIA achieves a score of 0.6272 on HealthBench Professional, demonstrating that architectural design and engine-level reliability are critical factors for clinical LLM performance beyond simple prompt engineering.

---

[CP-Agent: A Calibrated Risk-Controlled Agent for Feedback-Driven Competitive Programming](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24693)

- CP-Agent: introduces a calibrated risk-controlled framework for competitive programming that utilizes Hypothesis Prober, Solution Validator, Test Augmentation, and Experience Memory to transform execution feedback into measurable performance gains without parameter updates.
- The framework models feedback-driven solving as a calibrated stopped process, using a frozen controller manifest to audit decisions on held-out traces and ensure post-selection validity.
- CP-Agent achieves significant improvements in Pass@1 and Refine@5 metrics across multiple LLM backbones by effectively orchestrating verification tools and leveraging historical repair experiences.

---

[Emotional intelligence in large language models is fragmented across perception, cognition, and interaction](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24686)

- FACET (Functional Affective Competence and Empathy Test): introduces a psychometrically grounded framework to evaluate the structural integrity of emotional intelligence in LLMs across Perception, Cognition, and Interaction.
- The framework utilizes a dual-metric approach, combining objective accuracy scores for perceptual and cognitive tasks with Elo-based subjective ratings for interactive performance.
- Evaluation of nine frontier LLMs reveals that emotional intelligence is not a monolithic capability but is fragmented, with models often decoupling robust objective recognition from effective interactive empathy.

---

[How Many Tools Should an LLM Agent See? A Chance-Corrected Answer](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24660)

- BoR (Bits-over-Random): introduces a chance-corrected evaluation metric and reinforcement learning reward signal to dynamically determine the optimal number of tools to present to an LLM agent per query.
- The framework utilizes a Markov Decision Process where an RL agent observes tool relevance scores and registry size to decide between stopping or continuing to expand the tool shortlist.
- By optimizing for selectivity rather than fixed-depth recall, the approach reduces distractor load and improves downstream tool-choice accuracy for LLMs across various benchmarks.

---

[IterInject: Indirect Prompt Injection Against LLM Agents via Feedback-Guided Iterative Optimization](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24659)

- IterInject: introduces a feedback-guided iterative framework for indirect prompt injection that closes the loop between injection, diagnosis, and refinement using a Disguise Seed Bank, Feedback Diagnoser, Payload Optimizer, and Disguise Synthesizer.
- The framework employs a rule-based diagnoser to produce structured feedback, which an LLM-based optimizer uses to refine payloads conditioned on the full optimization history.
- Mechanistic analysis identifies an attention-mediated threshold mechanism in mid-to-late layers, where payload attention competes for resources until a critical threshold is crossed, enabling successful hijacking.

---

[Agent-as-Peer-Debriefer: A Multi-Agent Framework with Perspective-Based Refinement for Qualitative Analysis](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24600)

- Agent-as-Peer-Debriefer: introduces a multi-agent framework for LLM-assisted qualitative data analysis that simulates human peer debriefing to improve coding quality through perspective-based refinement.
- The framework utilizes a Hierarchical Coding Agent to generate initial codes and self-reflection memos, which are then refined by three Peer-Debriefing Agents using Theory-Driven, Data-Driven, or Applied perspectives.
- Experimental results across three datasets demonstrate that perspective-based refinement consistently improves alignment with human-annotated codes compared to single-LLM baselines.

---

[Hera: Learning Long-Horizon Coordination for Device–Cloud Collaborative LLM Agents](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24598)

- Hera: introduces a step-level device–cloud LLM agent coordinator that optimizes task success while minimizing cloud reliance through a two-stage training paradigm.
- The framework utilizes an imitation learning module for cold-start initialization and a reinforcement learning module with a state grouping mechanism to refine routing decisions for performance–cost co-optimization.
- Hera dynamically routes tasks between a lightweight device-side LLM agent and a powerful cloud-side LLM agent, achieving high success rates with significantly reduced cloud usage.

---

[PEDESTRIANQA: A Benchmark for Vision-Language Models on Pedestrian Intention and Trajectory Prediction](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24562)

- PedestrianQA: introduces a multimodal video-based dataset that formulates pedestrian intention and trajectory prediction as question-answering tasks augmented with structured rationales.
- The framework utilizes a Metadata Aggregator, VLM Motion Captioner, and LLM-API Prompting Engine to generate high-quality, fine-grained, object-grounded natural language explanations for pedestrian behavior.
- The Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct baseline model, finetuned on this dataset, demonstrates significant improvements in intention classification, trajectory forecasting, and rationale quality across both structured and unstructured traffic environments.

---

[Emission-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Sustainable Electric Vehicle Charging and Carbon Dioxide Reduction Under Varying Renewable Penetration](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24543)

- EV2Gym: introduces an emission-aware reinforcement learning strategy for sustainable EV charging that utilizes a SAC Agent, a multi-objective Reward Function, and a comprehensive State Representation to balance grid compliance with carbon reduction.
- The framework integrates a Transformer Model and Charger Model to enforce realistic grid constraints while optimizing charging schedules based on real-time carbon intensity and renewable energy availability.
- By employing an EV Battery Model with two-stage charging dynamics, the approach enables high-fidelity benchmarking of smart charging strategies under varying renewable penetration scenarios.

---

[DemoEvolve: Overcoming Sparse Feedback in Agentic Harness Evolution with Demonstrations](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24539)

- DemoEvolve: introduces a demonstration-bootstrapped approach to harness evolution that uses competent human trajectories as reference experience for a coding proposer to diagnose and edit task harnesses for frozen LLMs.
- The framework addresses the credit-assignment problem in long-horizon stochastic environments where sparse feedback often leads to noisy selection of non-causal harness edits.
- By providing positive state-action counterfactuals, DemoEvolve enables the coding proposer to localize missing state abstractions and improve performance more effectively than self-rollout or text-guided methods.

---

[Is Decentralized AI Governable? From Regulative Policy to Constitutive Protocol](http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.24538)

- DeAI: introduces a six-layer decentralizing stack to analyze how partial decentralization across model weights, training, compute, harness, identity, and ownership compounds into a governance vacuum.
- The paper argues that traditional governance through normative address fails in decentralized systems due to accountability and incapacitation gaps, necessitating a shift toward protocol-based architectural constraint.
